They have sex—Feyre feeling the bond settle between them. She asks what happens now. Rhys says anything she wants: they can have the bond verified, get married, or do nothing yet. There’s no rush. He tells her they leave in the morning because the mortal queens have finally responded. Feyre glows faintly with a Day Court power after accepting the bond. They sleep together often—Rhys explains that it’s normal for new mates, part instinct, part magic. When she brings up children, Rhys says she is ''never'' expected to bear anything. They agree they don’t want kids now—they want time to live, adventure, and just be together. They have forever.

They winnow back to the Illyrian camp. Cassian immediately provokes Rhys into a fistfight just to work off the tension. When they later return to Velaris, Mor, Cassian, Azriel, and Amren all bow to Feyre.

Three of the [[Mortal Queens|mortal queens]] arrive; the other three refused to come because they were offended last time. Mor presents the [[Veritas]] orb, and Rhys shows them [[Velaris]]. The queens say they’ll “consider it,” though Rhys senses they’ve already decided no.  Ones of the queens questions Rhys’s motives. Nesta snaps, begging them for help so humans don’t die in the coming war. Cassian steps to her side, wipes away her tear, and vows to fight for her people. The queens leave—but the young queen secretly leaves behind a box containing their half of the Book. Her note says she believes them, warns them not to trust the other queens, and reveals the “sick” [[Vassa|sixth queen]] was never ill. Rhys offers Nesta and Elain safety in Velaris. Nesta lets Elain choose, and Elain says she cannot leave.

Back in Velaris, they give Amren the second half of the Book. Feyre thanks Cassian for supporting Nesta; she says Nesta never forgets kindness. Suddenly, dark winged creatures (like the Attor) descend on Velaris—the queens have betrayed them. One drops the golden-haired queen’s corpse, impaled on a lamppost. Feyre attacks, using the skills she learnt from training with Cassian, she fights to defend the city, also using her powers to shape wolves and other creatures of water to kill. She spots the [[Attor]] fleeing and goes after it. Ignoring Rhys’s pleas through the bond, Feyre winnows above the Attor, fires ash arrows into its wings, and wraps around it as living flame. She kills it—“for Rhys, for Clare, and for me”—and winnows away before it crashes.

Velaris is secured. Amren finishes deciphering the Book and says Feyre must touch the Cauldron and speak the spell to nullify its power. But joining the two Book halves would release a blast strong enough to be felt across the world and attract enemies. They plan to infiltrate Hybern’s stronghold, with Feyre going in while Rhys stays outside.

Feyre and Rhys choose to share a bedroom. He gives her [[Rhysand's Mother's Ring|his mother’s ring]]—the one Feyre retrieved from the Weaver. He explains his mother left it with the Weaver to ensure that any future mate or bride would be strong enough to win it back. Feyre promises that after the Cauldron is nullified, she wants everything: a wedding, the bond confirmed, and a celebration. 

They infiltrate Hybern’s castle and locate the Cauldron. The Cauldron whispers to Feyre, urging her to join the Book halves. She does—and just as she prepares to speak the nullifying spell, [[Jurian]] appears. Rhys grabs the Book from Feyre, and hides it. Jurian demands to know where Miryam is; when they say she’s dead, he calls it a lie. Mor admits Miryam is alive, mated and married to Drakon. They try to winnow out, but the Cauldron’s power blocks every escape route.  The King arrives. Jurian shoots Azriel with an ash bolt laced with Bloodbane. The King threatens to kill him unless they cooperate. He leads them to the throne room, where Tamlin and Lucien are waiting. He says, “I upheld my end of the bargain. Now uphold yours.”

Tamlin bargained with Hybern: if the King returned Feyre to him, Tamlin would open the Spring Court to Hybern’s armies and let them tear down the wall. He also agreed Feyre would work for the King. Tamlin holds out his hand like she’s a pet. Feyre refuses. The King insists she will obey once she sees “the final part.” He orders the bond between Feyre and Tamlin broken. Feyre begs Tamlin to think, but he insists she’s “not well” and claims the bond has manipulated her. Feyre promises she’ll go with him if he lets her friends go. Rhys tries to hold her through the bond and Tamlin finally realizes they are mates.

Feyre warns that if Tamlin takes her, she’ll destroy everything he loves, but he brushes it off. The King says she’ll comply—and the mortal queens enter dragging her bound, gagged sisters. The queens have sided with Hybern. The King promised to make them immortal using the Cauldron. Ianthe revealed the Feyre's sisters as “perfect test subjects.”

The King blasts Cassian’s wings to shreds. He throws Elain into the Cauldron; she emerges High Fae. Nesta is shoved in next—coming out changed in a way Feyre can sense is different. Feyre vomits through this, feeling her sisters change. 

Lucien suddenly realizes Elain is his mate.

Then Feyre breaks free and uses her power, pretending Rhys had warped her mind. Through the bond she begs Rhys to play along and get her sisters out. Rhys pretends she has broken free of his control. Feyre demands the King sever all bonds between herself and Rhys—mating bond and bargain included. She turns to Tamlin, begging him to release her friends and just take her home. Tamlin says he doesn’t care if she’s mated; she’s his. The King breaks the bond. Feyre faints and wakes to find her tattoo gone. Mor winnows her sisters to safety before Tamlin can take them. Rhys, Cassian, and Azriel escape as well. Tamlin takes Feyre “home” to the Spring Court.

Feyre plays along, telling Tamlin she can help him track down Rhysand, the Inner Circle, and even get her sisters back. She can tell Lucien is already suspicious. But inside, Feyre is calm—this is her plan. She will take the Spring Court down from within.

Tamlin has no idea he has just brought the High Lady of the Night Court into his home.

### ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]''
Feyre returns to the Spring Court to spy for Rhysand and the Night Court to gather information. She welcomes the Hybern delegation made up of Jurian and the twin nephews of the King: the [[Prince]] s [[Dagdan]] and [[Brannagh]].

They have gone on the supposed mission of inspecting the cracks in the wall, but it is later revealed that since they arrived, they were actually poisoning the residents of Tamlin's manor with [[Faebane]], in small doses so that it is not noticeable, but the effect is cumulative.

Feyre begins to gather all the information that could be useful, from plans to the number of troops and locations of attacks. During her stay, she tries to make Tamlin and Ianthe jealous by being close to Lucien and causing him to lose his faith in Tamlin by allowing the latter to hurt her during one of his emotional explosions of power. She also sowed discord among the residents of the manor, especially the sentries, by showing them that Tamlin would always side with Ianthe, and through her daemati powers she implants in them the idea that, after killing Dagdan and Brannagh she had to flee because Tamlin did nothing to stop them from trying to kill her. The news spread through the court and there is an internal rebellion that leads to Hybern's forces having no resistance when invading them. As a result of this, many a fae die and Hybern manages to attack other courts.

Then Feyre, accompanied by Lucien, makes her return trip to the Night Court but they had to cross the [[Autumn Court]] instead of the Summer Court since Feyre is wanted there. They are attacked by Lucien's brothers, [[Eris Vanserra]] and other [[Lucien's Half-brothers|two brothers]], at the request of their father, [[Beron Vanserra]], the High Lord of the court. They manage to flee from the brothers and cross the border into the [[Winter Court]] but are once again attacked by Lucien's brothers and are only saved when Cassian and Azriel come to their rescue and take them away to [[Velaris]] along with [[Morrigan]].

Once in Velaris she meets with Rhysand, and they begin to weigh their various possible allies. Then her mate sends letters to all the High Lords to schedule a meeting.

Feyre's sisters, Nesta and Elain are living in Velaris, recently being turned into [[High Fae]] with peculiar powers, especially Nesta, and as the King of Hybern wants back the power Nesta took from the Cauldron, he sends two of his [[Ravens]] to kidnap her, which they were unable to do and died trying.

During the attack on [[Adriata]], [[Varian]], the [[Prince]], sends a message to [[Amren]] warning her that the city is under attack by Hybern. Quickly Rhysand rallies his troops and alongside his Inner Circle, with the exception of Amren, march to the [[Summer Court]]. During the battle Feyre and Mor fight together to kill the Hybern soldiers who have invaded the [[Adriata Palace|palace]] and the nearby streets and Rhysand faces the King himself, who in the end turns out to be an apparition as he isn't really there. Hybern is defeated and when they go to speak to [[Tarquin]], the High Lord, he shows them he still holds a grudge from their last visit and tells them to leave his territory.

When the High Lords agree to hold the meeting, the event was at the [[Dawn Court Palace]], belonging to [[Thesan]], the High Lord of the [[Dawn Court]], as it is the place closest to [[the Middle]]. The meeting is attended by the seven High Lords with their delegations. It is revealed that Tamlin has not been working with Hybern, but in fact infiltrating their ranks for info to give the courts a chance at defeating him in the war. After several discussions and altercations, they agree to join forces against Hybern, with the exception of the Autumn Court who leaves after Feyre showed that when they revived her each of the High Lords gave her a little of their power unknowingly when she attacked the High Lord of the Autumn Court, injuring his wife at the process.

During the second day of the meeting, they hear a great roar and Nesta, who has a connection to the Cauldron, tells them that the King has used it to destroy the wall.

Tarquin tells them that by coming to their aid, he is nullifying the blood rubies he had sent them and offers Tamlin refuge for the citizens of his court and the enemy army invaded the Summer Court across the border with the Spring Court.

A battle ensued between Hybern and the joint armies of the Summer Court and the Night Court (the latter consisting of [[Illyrian]] s and the [[Darkbringers]] of the [[Hewn City]]) where the defenders of Prythian are victorious and at the end of the battle Tarquin drowns all the soldiers of Hybern who were still alive. After the conflict ends, they are informed that Hybern's next attack would be on the Winter Court, so they use a glamour on the armies so that it seems that they are still there and move to the Winter Court for the new battle.

This new battle is bloodier and narrowly won by the Prythian forces. In this fight [[Cassian]] is seriously injured.

After the battle, while in the war camp, the Cauldron deceives Elain, posing as her ex-fiancé [[Graysen]], and takes her to the Hybern camp, from where she is rescued, along with [[Briar]], by Feyre and Azriel with the help of Jurian (who proves he had never actually been on Hybern's side but is a spy) and Tamlin, who also happens to be a spy. Tamlin uses his beast form to keep Hybern's beasts at bay so the four can get away, also sending them a powerful blast of wind to help them escape, as Azriel's wings were torn. 

When they learn that the final battle would take place in the Mortal Lands to the south of the island, the High Lords agree to transfer as many mortals as possible to Adriata to save their lives. After that, the armies marched to the battlefield, where the [[Bone Carver]] and [[Bryaxis]] (recruited by Feyre) and [[Stryga]], the Weaver, (recruited by Rhys) also arrive to aid them in battle. A short time later, they are joined by the forces of the Spring Court and the Autumn Court, as well as an army of mortals led by Jurian and Graysen. During the course of the battle the King uses the force of the Cauldron thus killing many defenders of Prythian (among them the Bone Carver) and soldiers of his own army. When it seems that their forces would be overwhelmed, reinforcements arrive from [[Cretea]], the island of [[Drakon]] and [[Miryam]] and also mortals from the Continent led by Mr. Archeron and [[Vassa]], the sixth mortal queen mentioned in the previous book.

Knowing that the only way to win is by nullifying the Cauldron's powers, Nesta plans a distraction for the King to take him away from the magic item so Feyre and Amren can get closer to it. When they do, Amren tells Feyre to place her hands on the Cauldron and she does, the Cauldron shows her the vision of what is happening across the battle,  showing how the King uses her father as a human shield and then breaks his neck. Also how, when he is about to kill Nesta and Cassian, Elain emerges from behind him and stabs him in the neck with [[Truth-Teller|Azriel's knife]] and then Nesta uses the knife to cut off his head.

When the vision ends, Feyre tries to kill Amren for betraying her, but she explains that only her true form can kill all the remaining Hybern soldiers, who continue to fight despite their King having died. She gets into the Cauldron and Feyre reads the spell from the [[Book of Breathings]] to wield the [[Cauldron]] and free her true form. During the process the Cauldron explodes, and she emerges in her true form of fire and light and takes out all remaining enemies.

Because the Cauldron is tied to the very foundations of their world, its destruction triggers a vacuum that begins to unmake reality itself. To stop the unraveling, Feyre Remakes the Cauldron, using the final spell Amren taught her from the Book of Breathings. Rhysand stands behind her and pours every his power into her, and Feyre channels that power and her own, through herself. But it requires so much that Rhysand gives everything—until there is nothing left and he dies in the process. After, Feyre begs them, the other High Lords gave a seed of their power to revive him as they had done with her in [[Under the Mountain]]. Tamlin hesitates, but upon seeing Feyre so distraught, he gives his seed of power to Rhysand to resurrect him, stating "Be happy, Feyre." Rhys comes back to life, bringing Amren, now newly High Fae, with him from between life and death. 

A short time later Feyre and her sisters attend a small service in honor of their father and then a meeting called by Feyre in the ruins of the Archeron's estate to renegotiate a new [[Treaty]] between Fae and mortals.

She was last seen drinking at Velaris with her friends and later flying over the city with Rhysand.

### ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]''
After the end of the [[The War with Hybern|war]] against Hybern, Feyre fully assumes her role as High Lady, helping to rebuild Velaris and working with Rhysand to maintain old alliances and forge new ones between the Faerie and mortal realms to try to keep the peace. She helps many charities and organizations that arise in the city with the mission of returning it to its previous state. While she is in the [[Rainbow of Velaris]] trying to help she sees a [[Polina's Studio|studio]] that has not yet been rebuilt. A faerie called [[Ressina]] sees her looking, approaches her and tells her that it belonged to her friend, [[Polina]], who did not survive the attack. She then tells her that the artists do not forget that she came to their aid when Hybern attacked and then invites her to come to her own [[Ressina's Studio|studio]] where one night a week several artists gather to paint together. Feyre is indecisive as she does not want to be seen painting and in the end she decides to do it alone in Polina's studio.

Meanwhile [[Winter Solstice]] is approaching, a great celebration at the Night Court, and also her 21st birthday. As it is customary to give gifts for Solstice, Feyre and Elain go shopping, during which they meet [[Aranea]] and her life story. Feyre also tries to get closer to Nesta, who after the war has moved into her own [[Nesta's Apartment|apartment]], like Lucien, and has distanced herself from the family preferring living her life on her own, something that saddens both Feyre and Elain.

When Feyre finally decides to buy Polina's studio, her [[Polina's Sister|sister]] gives it to her but does not take her money and together with Ressina she opens a [[Feyre's Studio|studio]] to teach painting for free to all those who would like to learn.

### ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]''
Feyre is dealing with the aftermath of the war and is shown in the [[Feyre and Rhysand's Bonus Chapter|bonus chapter]] that she continues to have nightmares and deal with her lingering trauma. She has also continued to develop a new Treaty, Cassian notes that she, Amren, and Rhys had spent months working on it, but Vallahan is said to not want to sign. 

She is also struggling with Nesta, who has been traumatized by her experiences and is spiraling, and is using Feyre's money to drink herself into oblivion. Feyre and the rest of the Inner Circle give Nesta an ultimatum to either go to the [[House of Wind]]  to train with Cassian and work in [[The Library|the library]] or leave for the Mortal Lands. Feyre is shown deeply worried about Nesta's destroying herself in the year since the war and they made this plan to help Nesta learn to control herself, as Feyre says.

Shortly after, Feyre becomes pregnant with a baby boy. While [[Madja]] tells her that her pregnancy will be dangerous, she doesn't know that since he will have Illyrian wings, and her body isn't made to give birth to an Illyrian, Feyre and the baby will die. Rhys keeps this information from her for a couple weeks as he is hoping he can find a way to save both Feyre and the child. 

One day, Nesta gets very angry and tells Feyre that she and her son will die from childbirth. Feyre is distraught to learn not just that her son's life is at risk but that Rhys, Amren, and everyone had not told her the full extent of the danger. Nesta feels awful about how she told Feyre in a rage and spoke in anger and eventually apologizes to Feyre in her thoughts, which Feyre's daemati abilities allow her to hear. Feyre tells her she forgives her.

One night as Spring dawns in Velaris and Feyre was two months away from giving birth, Rhysand sees a rare red star blasting across the sky while with Feyre. Sarah J. Mass confirmed this red star was ''[[w:c:throneofglass:Aelin Galathynius|Aelin Galathynius]]'' as she falls through darkness and passes through worlds, one of which is Prythian after using the wyrdkeys to open the wyrdgates in ''[[:w:c:throneofglass:Kingdom of Ash|Kingdom of Ash]]''. Rhysand actually sees her falling but he's "weirded out by it" so he claims he saw a red star. Rhys senses she's in trouble and reaches with his [[magic]] to slow down her fall.

When [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]] and [[Emerie]] are in the Blood Rite, an Illyrian Warrior helps them. Stating that "Feyre Cursebreaker would not forgive me for killing her sister."

Feyre gives birth early and she dies, her baby born still. However, Nesta uses the objects of The [[Dread Trove]] to stop time, promising the [[Cauldron]] the powers she stole back in exchange for their lives. During her bargain with the Cauldron, Nesta has it change the shape of Feyre's and her own anatomy so they can survive Illyrian births in the future. Feyre is healthy again and she and Rhysand name the baby, [[Nyx]]. Feyre and her mate are happy and looking forward to a life with the little boy.

### ''[[w:c:crescentcity:House of Sky and Breath (novel)|House of Sky and Breath]]''
After [[w:c:crescentcity:Bryce Quinlan|Bryce Quinlan]] manages to escape the [[w:c:crescentcity:Asteri|Asteri]] by opening a gate to travel through the worlds to reach [[w:c:crescentcity:Hel|Hel]] in order to request aid from the Princes, she accidentally lands somewhere in Velaris near the [[Sidra River]] instead, carrying with her the [[Gwydion|Starsword]]. Azriel finds her, blindfolds her, and flies her to a residence, carrying her to the living room. Bryce is there for a few moments with Azriel, Amren, Cassian and Nesta, until Feyre and Rhysand arrive. Feyre enters the room first wearing a white loose shirt and brown pants splattered with paint, Rhysand entering after her. Bryce pleads her case to Amren, and she translates to the others. Feyre then asks Bryce for her name, and Amren translates the question. Bryce addresses Rhysand and Feyre noticing they possess an air of authority and tells them her name. Rhysand steps forward, smiling slightly, and introduces himself in the Old Language saying: "Hello, Bryce Quinlan. My name is Rhysand".

### ''[[w:c:crescentcity:House of Flame and Shadow (novel)|House of Flame and Shadow]]''
When Nesta uses [[the Mask]] to defeat a [[Middengard Wyrm]] she has a hard time breaking out of its hold so [[Azriel]] tries to help her snap out of it by mentioning all the people that love Nesta. He mentions Feyre, Elain, and Nyx along with Nesta's mate and her friends, and says they are waiting for her.

Later, [[w:c:crescentcity:Ember Quinlan|Ember Quinlan]] and [[w:c:crescentcity:Randall Silago|Randall Silago]] stayed in the [[Night Court]]. Ember tried to explain to [[Rhysand]] and Azriel that Nesta wasn't at fault for Bryce taking their weapons to [[w:c:crescentcity:Midgard|Midgard]]. She says she thinks only Feyre believed her and Feyre had warned Rhysand to stop berating her.

## Appearance
Feyre is slender, with pale skin, golden-brown hair, and slightly up-tilted blue-grey eyes. Before arriving at the Spring Court, Feyre ate very little due to her family's poverty, rendering her extremely thin. However, once settled into the Spring Court, she filled out to a healthy weight, as she says herself her body now looks like the one of a woman, with her full breasts and the sweeping, soft curves of her waist and hips. Despite her thinness, she had always been considered strong, likely from her hunting throughout her childhood.                                        

After her time in the Spring Court, her skin tanned and freckles dust her face. She has a pert, pretty and straight nose, sharp cheekbones, and a wide, full mouth (her father's mouth). She is described as beautiful, even as a human Lucien and Tamlin are shocked at how nicely she cleans up.                                        

After turning into a High Fae while living with Tamlin, she wastes away from the trauma she endured Under the Mountain and becomes skinny once more, during this period she describes her face as gaunt, with sharp cheekbones, her eyes are dull and smudged with purple beneath, her full lips are wan, and her collarbones jut out. She eventually regains her weight during her stay in the Night Court in ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'' and is described as exquisite. 

#### '''Tattoos:'''

* Her left forearm bears a tattoo given to her by Rhysand from their deal made Under the Mountain. The tattoo acts as a bargain contract that requires Feyre to spend one week every month of her life in the Night Court.
* The tattoo that signifies her bargain with Rhysand is later destroyed by the King of Hybern in ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]''. However, her right arm now bears a matching tattoo to the original, marking her as the High Lady of the Night Court.
* After the events of ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]'', she has a black band from her bargain with Bryaxis on her left arm. and above it, a tattoo that marks her bargain to die at the same time as Rhysand. She has four phases of the moon with a star in the center of them tattooed down her spine, for her deal with the [[Bone Carver]].
* In ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'' she asks Rhys to replace the eye that is tattooed on the center of her palms with the design of the Night Court insignia, a mountain with three stars. This kind of marking can never be altered.

## Personality
Feyre is hardworking and dedicated to her family, spending most of her days providing and caring for her [[Feyre's Father|father]] and sisters by hunting and selling animals at the market. Despite her willingness to protect her family at all costs, she feels great hatred for the Fae, as a result of her upbringing, her surroundings, and earlier experiences of spending time at the [[Spring Court]]. She is very stubborn and has a sarcastic sense of humor that is usually not seen. She is very confident and selfless, willing to sacrifice anything for the people she loves. 

She loves music, dance, and art, and she sees the world in vivid colors, light, and shadows. She is also very understanding, as she understands Rhysand and his problems, and she understands why he did what he did, and why he does what he does. During her time in the [[House of Wind]], she developed a love for reading, comfort she found through characters and their stories as she dealt with her trauma and treatment at the [[Spring Court]]. 

Feyre is seductive and charming when she wants to be but generally prefers to remain open to the people she has an affection for. She hates it when others judge her and is fairly indignant in terms of those who don't receive equal rights. She can be very cunning, an example of this was when she defeated the [[Middengard Wyrm]] with strategy alone, without physical strength or magical powers. She is brilliant at masking her emotions, lying, and playing different roles, and has a quick temper when provoked. She has unwavering loyalty towards those who have earned her trust and hatred towards those who have broken it. In addition to compassion and mercy, like the time he saved young Briar, who was being tortured in Hybern's war camp.

Feyre has a dark side that she doesn't mind showing, driven forward by her compassion, determination, self-sacrificing nature, and faith in those she loves. Feyre is also a keen artist. She often used art as a form of escape and entertainment. Although for a period after the traumas of Under the Mountain, she stopped painting, she slowly managed to recover after living in Velaris and began painting again.

## Powers and Abilities
When Feyre died [[Under the Mountain]], she was resurrected and reborn by the combined powers of the seven [[High Lord|High Lords]]. She was [[Made]] into a High Fae, thus gaining the physique of High Fae, their strength and speed, their keener senses, and also their immortality. [[Rhysand]] notes that she is stronger than a regular [[High Fae]] and is demonstrating the signs that are seen in a High Lord's Heir. He warns her that she needs to train and learn to control it or it will destroy her, also to protect herself as power so great could make her a target for breeding. 

Feyre inherited kernals of power from all seven High Lord's powers, representative of all the land of [[Prythian]], described by [[Helion]] as ''“So small—like a fish missing a single scale,"'' yet also ''"It seems she took far more than that, if she could be within seconds of drowning Beron despite the wards."'' These powers give her control over all elements, as well as sub-skills such as shapeshifting, healing,  and [[daemati]] abilities. 

Feyre is able to track hidden objects connected to the High Lords and bypass their magic as she has their “fingerprints” which allowed her to do such things through that power and use those abilities together, she also has a connection to the High Lords, whereby being in their presence and land can heighten that specific power. As she is [[Made]], she also a connection to the [[Cauldron]] and is able to sense it with her sisters in [[A Court of Wings and Ruin|A Court of Wings and Ruin.]] Feyre is able to winnow from one place to another, is skilled with glamour to the point of being able to show an illusion of the Night Court army, can access the pocket realm, and use faelights among other High Fae abilities. Furthermore, Feyre's blood has healing abilities, given to Rhysand in [[A Court of Mist and Fury]] when he is captured and injured. 

She receives [[Illyrian]] combat training from [[Cassian]] and Illyrian flight training from [[Azriel]]. She also learned to cast unique and old spells through her training with [[Amren]]. Two of the most notable were drawn from the [[Book of Breathings]], which later revealed themselves to be written in [[Leshon Hakodesh]] (the same language as Bryce Quinlan's tattoo)—power that Feyre ultimately wields to help Remake the [[Cauldron]].

Feyre is able to wield the [[Cauldron]]. Feyre’s first attempt to use the spell from the Book of Breathings was when she placed her hand on the Cauldron. However, Amren had lied about their plan. Feyre was left connected and exposed, alone in that bond as the Cauldron pulled her through the battlefield and showed her what was happening. When it ended and the Cauldron pulled back into itself Feyre then becomes a conduit to the Cauldron. Feyre used the Book’s spell to wield the Cauldron's magic and unbind Amren.  

The following list comprises Feyre's abilities and their corresponding courts as well as her other abilities: 

*'''[[Spring Court]]:''' Shapeshifting. She is able to manifest claws, talons, and Illyrian wings, and can shift the nature of her eyes to be able to see in the dark. She is also able to impersonate other people, like [[Ianthe]].
*'''[[Summer Court]]:''' Water Manipulation. She controls water expertly and can shape it however she wants like butterflies and birds, and was able to create "[[Water-wolves|water wolves]]" that can drown Hybern soldiers with ease. She uses it against [[Beron Vanserra|Beron]], the High Lord of Autumn after he insults Rhysand and trapped him in an "all-element" sphere, as well as drowned and choke a Hybern soldier in Adriatic with a blast of water. She is also able to control her tears as she cried seawater tears to fool Lucien.
*'''[[Autumn Court]]:''' Fire Manipulation. She first loses control in a nightmare and submerges her bedroom in fire. Eventually, she masters control of fire and blast it at enemies and summon it in her hand without it burning her to burn a paintbrush, her fire magic was seen being able to shred through Hybern magic shields, controlled it to burn in their throats and behead Hybern soldiers, and burn a forest clearing during training as well as burn flesh as she’d grievously injured Brannagh and also accidentally burned the [[Lady of the Autumn Court|Lady of Autumn]] during her temperamental fight with Beron, much to the chagrin of Beron. She also uses it as she fights the [[Attor]] mid-flight. 
*'''[[Winter Court]]:''' Ice Manipulation. She masters control of ice, as seen when she froze the water she’d sprayed on the Attor-like creatures during the Battle of Velaris and watched them shatter upon slamming into the cobblestone, using it to refreeze parts of a lake after [[Eris Vanserra|Eris]] melts it. [[Kallias]] asks her if she has mastered it, to which she replies she has.
*'''[[Dawn Court]]:''' Healing Abilities (through her blood).
*'''[[Day Court]]:''' Air Manipulation which is her most used power under Day Court magic as she has created barriers of hard air for protection and to break her fall and those barriers can block scents and manipulate wind to carry burned ashes of the paintbrush from her hand and then controlled it to carry rosy scents from the garden outside into the room. Light Generation, she can produce white blinding light as she did in Hybern’s castle Spell-Cleaving and, she can use the white light of day to cleave through wards and dismantle spells. She has control of these abilities as well, using them several times throughout the series.
*'''[[Night Court]]:''' Darkness Manipulation, she has been seen being able to produce pools of darkness as well as banish light by summoning it in a room during a battle with Hybern soldiers in Adriatia to blind them.  She also has [[Daemati]] abilities where she can read people’s thoughts and project her own through to into other people’s minds, she can alter and control people’s memories and perceptions and emotions as she did with Tarquin as she’d manipulated his perceptions of her and made him feel safe around her. She also does this when she broke into the mind of a Spring Court sentry and planted false memories to manipulate him in her grand scheme to undermine Tamlin’s sovereignty and weakens his court, as he was believed to be in alliance with Hybern. She does it again with Ianthe by breaking into her mind and compelling her actions to smash her own hand, twisting her mind so she would always live in fear. She has also show the ability to mind hop as she hopped into Lucien’s mind and saw through his eyes. She Has mastery of these abilities due to her training with Rhysand. These abilities are strengthened after she mates with 
*'''[[Winnowing]]:''' Feyre is able to travel far distances. She is able to winnow others along with her. Notably, she winnows on top of the Attor in midair, fighting and killing it.

### Abilities

* '''[[Cauldron|Cauldron Wielding]]:''' After mastering [[Ouroboros]] Amren tells Feyre that she is strong enough to control the Cauldron on her own and wield its power. In [[A Court of Wings and Ruin]] she wields the Cauldron's magic to set Amren free. ''"I cupped my hands as if the cracked thirds of the Cauldron could fit into them. The entire universe into the palm of my hand."'' The [[King of Hybern]] is the only other character to successfully wield the Cauldron.
*'''[[Unmade|Making/Unmaking]]:''' In [[A Court of Wings and Ruin]], during the final battle, Feyre Remakes the [[Cauldron]] after it breaks into three pieces: ''"I had broken it. I could remake it again...Remake the Cauldron. Forge it anew."'' She wields a spell from the [[Book of Breathings]], imbuing it with her power and [[Rhysand]]'s power channeled through her. However, Feyre has not Made anything since then. 
*'''[[Book of Breathings]]''': Feyre can wield the spells from the Book of Breathings. The Book itself is a powerful artifact forged by faeries who feared the Cauldron’s might, created from the last of the molten ore once used to forge the [[Cauldron]]. Its dark metal plates are bound by rings of gold, silver, and bronze, and its text is written in the [[Leshon Hakodesh]] (the same language as the Tattoo's on [https://crescentcity.fandom.com/wiki/Bryce_Quinlan Bryce]'s back). The spells within are designed to nullify the Cauldron’s power or command it completely. [[Amren]] believes Feyre can wield the spells in the ''Book of Breathings'' because Feyre is powerful enough and more importantly [[Made]] (reforged), which allows her to act as a conduit for its magic and, consequently, its counter-spells. 
*'''Art''': Feyre is an exceptional artist. She had been painting since she was child, even her [[Feyre's Mother|mother]] commented that one day it could secure her a match. Suggesting that she was likely showing immense talent at a young age. [[Cassian]] comments on how she had a way of capturing the world that always made him pause, that her paintings sometimes unsettled him. He says her painting of [[Ramiel]] was a near-perfect, real-life rendering, he also had to "fight back tears" when she gifted him one. [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]] had stared and stared at her portrait and cried in Feyre's arms when she saw it. Both [[Tamlin]] and [[Rhysand]] comment on how beautiful her paintings are.
* '''Illyrian Training''': Feyre is an exceptional warrior, trained by Cassian and Azriel. She’s capable of using swords, daggers, bow and arrow, or fighting hand to hand. Feyre has shown expert proficiency in swordsmanship, knifemanship, hand to hand combat, and of course, her bow and arrow. As seen in [[A Court of Mist and Fury]] when she rescues Rhysand, the Battle of Adriata, the battle in Velaris, and as she successfully defeats hundred year old Hybern Generals in combat with fighting and winnowing in [[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]. 
* '''Hunting and Tracking''': From a young age Feyre has always been an exceptional hunter and tracker. She learnt from watching others in her village and spent years practicing and honing those skills to feed and take of her family. She consistently uses these skills in [[A Court of Thorns and Roses]] when she is faced with creatures of [[Prythian]], or when she has to track down the [[Suriel]]. Most notably, she uses her wits to defeat the [[Middengard Wyrm]] during the trials [[Under the Mountain]] as a human.

## Relationships
### [[Rhysand]]


Feyre's first impression of Rhysand was that he was the most beautiful male she had ever seen. Once she gets to know Rhys, the image Tamlin had painted of him - of a cruel, self-involved bastard - begins to crack. Rhysand's consistent support and assistance Under The Mountain leads Feyre to almost trust him. He keeps her from shattering emotionally in the second trial and lost the usual calm pretense he normally has while he tries to save her in the third. Their relationship is built upon the foundations laid in ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]'': she was the one he revealed all his frustrations to, and she confessed her anguish to him at the end of the book.  The two share an understanding of one another, as well as a love for playful banter.

In ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'' Rhysand rescues Feyre from the Spring Court. He respects her freedom of choice and ability to defend herself; in her darkest times, he encourages her to strengthen her will and to exercise her autonomy. Rhysand struggles with Feyre's feelings of self-hate and eventually helps her to deal with them. Their relationship deepens and they develop romantic feelings for each other. Those feelings - along with some intense sexual desires for one another - continue evolving and eventually they learn that they are mates. After accepting the mating bond, Rhysand makes Feyre the High Lady of the Night Court.

In ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]'', the two are shown to miss each other very much while Feyre is in the Spring Court; they share a tearful reunion when she returns to the Night Court. Feyre has found her family with Rhys' Inner Circle, and they hold her in just as high a regard as they do their High Lord. Throughout the book, the two are shown to be very loving and protective of each other, and both are shown to tease and flirt with each other constantly. After the final battle ended, when Rhysand sacrificed himself to put the Cauldron back together, Feyre was devastated. She demanded the other High Lords to bring him back the way they did for her. At the end of the book, the two make a bargain to die at the same time so they are always together.

In ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'' both Feyre and Rhysand are stressed out from trying to sort things out after the War. Despite having previously decide to wait a while before entering motherhood, the devastation of the War on Velaris makes her second-guess just how confident she is that she has all the time in the world. After the Winter Solstice party, while being intimate, Feyre uses her daemati powers to show Rhys the image of their son that the Bone Carver had showed her, and she reveals that she is ready to have a baby with Rhys. 

In ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', Feyre is pregnant with Nyx. The couple is ecstatic at this, although Rhys does become more protective of Feyre, making an air shield around her which prevents anyone from touching her. However, when the danger of Feyre's pregnancy is revealed, Rhys is very worried for Feyre and Nyx's lives, so much so that he goes to Drakon and Miryam, the Dawn Court and even begs Helion on his knees to find something to save Feyre's life. When Feyre finds out that Rhys and the Inner Circle kept the danger of the pregnancy from her, she is very upset with Rhys, although later on, it seems that the two of them have resolved that issue. When Feyre is dying during the birth, Rhys is distraught but listens to Feyre in saying they need to risk themselves with Feyre being cut open to try to save Nyx . When Nesta saves Feyre and Nyx, he goes onto his knees and thanks her. Afterwards, he is seen next to Feyre, looking visibly shaken.

### [[Tamlin]]
In ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]'', Feyre's hatred for the fae thaws as she becomes close with Tamlin, High Lord of the Spring Court. Their romantic relationship is passionate.

In ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'' their relationship develops a rift when they return from Under the Mountain. Tamlin expects Feyre to go along with his political act: play the dutiful bride, honor tradition, and give the inhabitants of the Spring Court a reason to believe that peace has returned to the land. As a result of his trauma as well as a part of keeping up their image, Tamlin becomes extremely overprotective and controlling to the point of lashing out if she questions him and eventually locking her indoors. Being locked up causes Feyre to have a panic attack, leading to the discovery of her ability to manipulate shadows. She breaks off their engagement shortly before being rescued and taken to the Night Court. Due to the time that Feyre spends in the Night Court, certain feelings for Rhysand begin to awaken in her, which were later confirmed when she realized that they were made for each other, that they were mates. With Rhysand she is her own person, a happier person; not a mere consort, but instead the High Lady of the Night Court.

In ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]'', Feyre is shown to hold a lot of resentment for Tamlin, particularly for his involvement with Hybern and how her sisters were affected by it. The two's relationship after Feyre returns to the Night Court becomes hateful and strained, as seen at the High Lord's meeting, when he is trying to discredit and humiliate Feyre. However, when Feyre, Azriel, Elain and Briar are escaping from Hybern's camp, Tamlin helps Feyre escape. When the High Lords bring Rhys back to life, Feyre begs Tamlin to give a drop of his power to bring Rhys back, saying she will give him anything. He does so, telling Feyre to be happy. Afterwards, it seems that Feyre has forgiven Tamlin to some degree, with her also wishing Tamlin happiness. Regardless of this somewhat peace that was struck, she still holds resentment and distate for the High Lord of Spring. 

In ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'' and ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', Feyre is shown to not want anything to do with Tamlin. 

### [[Isaac Hale]]
Feyre had a distant, almost strictly sexual relationship with a boy from her village named Isaac Hale. He later goes on to marry [[Isaac Hale's Wife|a girl]] from the village.

### [[Lucien Vanserra]]
Lucien and Feyre have a rocky relationship. In ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]'', he dislikes her at first, being sarcastic and mocking towards her. Slowly, they start trusting each other and are seen joking around. He stands up for Feyre [[Under the Mountain]], helps her in her challenges and even risks his life to heal her. In ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'', he pleads with Tamlin to let Feyre train despite knowing it might cause Tamlin to abuse him. But after that he stays on the sidelines and does nothing to help her, no matter how much Feyre begs him to, and also tells Feyre to stop asking Tamlin for more. After Feyre is living in the Night Court, Lucien is sent by Tamlin to take her back. Even after Feyre tells him she doesn't want to return, he insists she can't give up and tries to take her back forcibly; Feyre is shown deeply wounded over his actions to force her back and then when she sees him in in Hybern.

Despite this and Lucien knowing Feyre had only pretended to be under Rhysand's control, when Feyre returns to the Spring Court, she and Lucien support each other, with Lucien defending Feyre when Tamlin attacks her and Feyre defending Lucien from Ianthe and the Hybern royals. Finally, Feyre brings Lucien along to the Night Court after he tells her he wishes to go and they leave the Spring Court. 

Their relationship doesn't fully recover as they both cannot understand each other and finally Lucien ends up joining Vassa and Jurian. Despite this, they remain friends with Feyre stating she wants Lucien to be happy and Lucien celebrating Feyre's birthday with her.

### [[Cassian]]
Cassian and Feyre have a close, sibling-like relationship. When they first meet, Cassian is angered at the treatment that Feyre had received from Tamlin, to the point that he had to restrain himself from going to the Spring Court and killing Tamlin himself. He jokes with her, trying to make her laugh. During dinner, Cassian and Feyre relate to each other because both know what it is like to go hungry for a night and to live in poverty. Feyre trains with Cassian. When Feyre has a breakdown in the middle of training, Cassian listens and doesn't condemn her actions from Under the Mountain until Rhys comes in and talks to Feyre. When Cassian's wings are shredded by the King of Hybern, Feyre is distraught. 

In ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]'', when Cassian and Azriel rescue Feyre and Lucien from Eris and Lucien's other brothers, Feyre cries out in joy upon seeing Cassian's wings healed. Cassian expresses his frustration that Feyre and Rhys kept her anointment as High Lady a secret from the court and that she surrendered herself to the Spring Court at the end of ''A Court of Mist and Fury''. Although he acknowledges that Feyre is capable of taking care of herself, he asks her to remember to rely on her court. Throughout the book, they continue to train together and maintain a close bond.

In ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'', before the Winter Solstice Party, Feyre and Cassian joke around, get drunk and try to decorate.  

In ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', Cassian and Feyre maintain a strong relationship, as after Cassian has a fight with Nesta, Feyre talks to him and makes him feel better. After Feyre discovers the risk of the pregnancy, she talks to Cassian mind-to-mind, and asks if he is okay, and she asks him to take care of Nesta. When Feyre gives birth, Cassian is shown to be very worried for Feyre.  

### [[Azriel]]
Feyre met Azriel when she arrived at the Night Court, after he was introduced as one of Rhysand's friends. At first, Feyre was uneasy and slightly afraid of him, but in the end, he becomes one of her closest friends. Azriel was the one who trained Feyre along with Cassian, and taught her how to fly. He even shares a smile with her after she makes a joke. He also feels free to bring out his dry sense of humor around her.

### [[Morrigan]]
When Tamlin locked Feyre in their house, Rhysand sensed Feyre's distress and sent his cousin Morrigan to rescue her. When she learned that the color red was a trigger for Feyre, Mor stopped wearing it. When Feyre was upset with Rhys for hiding that they were mates, she ran to Mor and asked her to take her far away from him. When Mor was having flashbacks to her family's abuse during a meeting in [[Hewn City]], Feyre sensed it and subtly nudged her in support, which Mor was grateful for. During the War, the two had become so close that Mor felt comfortable admitting her bisexuality to Feyre. Feyre calls Mor her closest friend and like a sister to her, and Mor is shown to staunchly defend and support Feyre, even against Rhys and Amren. Feyre and Mor often go out dancing and for drinks together. They especially enjoy making fun of the males of the Inner Circle together.

### [[Amren]]
Feyre and Amren have a good relationship. Amren tends to be blunt and honest with Feyre when she needs to be, but she does care deeply for Feyre. She is visibly angry and shaken when she finds out Feyre has left alone as a spy in the Spring Court. Likewise, Feyre is respectful and kind to Amren. During the Winter Solstice party, Feyre gives Amren a special folding carrier for her jigsaw puzzles, which earns her an eye roll and a smile of appreciation from Amren. Feyre also gives Amren a small ruby and silver brooch in the shape of angel wings, which earns her a small peck on the cheek. Feyre sometimes goes out drinking with Amren, like she does with Mor.

### [[Feyre's Father|Father]]
Feyre has a strained relationship with her father. She is somewhat angry that her father never found work after he was crippled and is ashamed of him. However, when her father shows up at the battle against Hybern sailing a fleet of ships, Feyre is shocked. She is distraught when the King of Hybern snapped his neck; she burns his body to honor him and later sets up a headstone. Despite her anger towards him, she does grieve him, At the end of ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', Feyre introduces Nyx to him. In a bonus chapter, it is revealed that Feyre used his name as a middle name for Nyx.

### [[Nesta Archeron]]
In a ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses|Court of Thorns and Roses]]'' The young huntress has a complicated relationship with her eldest sister, Nesta. For years, Nesta lashed out and belittled Feyre, fueled by anger toward their father’s inaction and resentment that Feyre was the one who stepped up to provide. She despised that Feyre could care for the family when their father could not, and often directed that bitterness at her youngest sister. As a result, Feyre came to believe Nesta was born with a sneer on her face, wondering if Nesta’s fixation on preserving her pride during their poverty might have helped them survive instead. After years of this treatment, Nesta’s voice became the one Feyre heard in her head whenever she felt insecure.

Despite this, Feyre never stopped caring for her sister. She warns Nesta that the man she hopes to marry is not right for her, a warning that Nesta receives with fury, shouting that no one will ever love Feyre. Yet when Feyre later returns from the Spring Court, she is stunned to learn that Nesta had gone to the Wall searching for her. Nesta also took Feyre’s advice and ends the relationship. As Nesta begins to reveal who she is beneath the anger, Feyre realizes that she misunderstood her sister, and the distance between them begins, slowly, to close. Throughout the rest of the series, Feyre maintains a deep understanding of who Nesta is beneath the pain. She loves and protects her sister fiercely. 

In ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'', Nesta is cold and unwelcoming to Feyre because she is upset that Feyre's status as High Fae will put their lives and Elain's engagement in danger, but after convincing from Elain she agrees to help Feyre defend the human lands. At the end of the book, she and Elain are forcibly turned into High Fae. Feyre is haunted by nightmares of her sisters being forced into the Cauldron, a fear that lingers long after the war.

In ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]'', Nesta is angry and resentful of Feyre because she blames her for her and Elain's transformation into High Fae. Over the course of the book, the two bond over their desires to protect the human lands and their shared concern over Elain. 

However, in ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'', their relationship becomes strained. she spends considerable time visiting Nesta and reaching out to her as Nesta spirals into anger, grief, and isolation. 

Feyre tries to persuade Nesta to come to the Winter Solstice party, but Nesta refuses. Nesta only agrees to come when Feyre says she will get her rent. When Nesta arrives, she wishes her a happy birthday but the two do not interact much further than that.

Feyre ultimately leads the effort and intervention in ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', determined to help her sister no matter how harsh Nesta’s resistance and anger becomes. 

The two's relationship is further strained when Nesta is forced to move into the House of Wind and train with Cassian. The two get into an argument and it ends with Nesta refusing to speak to her sister ever again. Feyre is shown talking to Cassian after and expressing how worried she is for Nesta and that she is trying to help her.  

After Nesta learns that the Inner Circle voted on whether she should be told she Made the weapons, she is furious, especially when she finds out Amren voted against telling her. She confronts Amren, and when Feyre attempts to step in and mediate, Nesta lashes out in anger, revealing that they had all kept it a secret that Feyre’s pregnancy could kill her. Feyre is visibly hurt and shaken by the revelation. However, she recognizes that Nesta is speaking from a place of pain and betrayal, and ultimately admits she is grateful to finally know the truth. Shortly after, Nesta apologizes to her. 

The two don't interact too much throughout the book, but their relationship does improve after Nesta begins to heal.  

When Feyre is dying while giving birth to Nyx, Nesta uses the [[Dread Trove]] to save Feyre from death. Nesta  strikes a bargain with the Cauldron. saying that she would give back the power she stole from it if it would save Feyre, Nyx, and Rhysand from death. Nesta tells Feyre she loves her, something she's never done before, and Feyre awakens, and tells Nesta she loves her too. The two then hug. A few weeks later, Nesta sees Feyre made a painting of her at the Pass of Enalius hanging in her house and cries. At the end of the book, two are seen together with Elain and Nyx, visiting their father's grave. 

### [[Elain Archeron]]
Feyre has a better relationship with her other sister Elain who is described as innocent and doesn't grasp things like her other sisters do. Feyre has a soft spot for Elain and buys her seeds for the flower garden that Elain tends to in the milder months whenever Feyre can afford it. In return, Elain gave Feyre a gift: three small pints of paint one summer. Feyre was very happy with her gift and painted her house with little vines and flowers along the windows and thresholds and edges of things, tiny curls of flame on the stones bordering the hearth. Any spare minutes Feyre had that summer she would bedeck their house in color, sometimes hiding clever decorations inside drawers, behind the threadbare curtains, underneath the chairs and table. In ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'', Elain still welcomes her into their home, even though she is High Fae. When Elain goes into a depression after being turned into High Fae, Feyre is shown to be very concerned for her. In ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'' and ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', the two remain close, forging a bond. Feyre often speaks of how she admires Elain's strength and spirit.  

### [[Nyx]]
Nyx is Feyre's newborn son. Even before he is born, Feyre was shown to love him very much, such as when she found out about the danger of her pregnancy, she chose not to panic so Nyx wouldn't feel those emotions. Even when it posed a risk to her life and Madja warns her Nyx likely wouldn't survive, she insisted on trying everything to save Nyx.

In a bonus chapter of House of Flame and Shadow, Nesta tells Bryce that Feyre is a perfect mother to Nyx.

## Trivia
* Feyre derives from "Fayre" which is an English name that means "beautiful".
** [[Sarah J. Maas]] stated in an interview that part of the reason she chose the name "Feyre" was because it sounded like "fair".
* Feyre has a lilac-and-pear scent according to Rhys. 
* Her father had a [[The Feyre|ship]] named after her, as did two others named after her sisters.
* She wears a silver ring with a star-sapphire that belonged to Rhysand’s mother, as a symbol of their mating bond.
* In [[Ouroboros]], the Mirror of Beginnings and Endings, Feyre sees her true self. She has black and gold fur, a ridge of dark scales down her back, enormous paws, blue-gray eyes, and a scaled tail.
* The first time she killed a living being, a young rabbit in the forest, Feyre was 14 and her family was starving. In the aftermath, she cried for hours, and she vomited that same rabbit after they had eaten it for dinner.
* Feyre is loosely based on Belle from Beauty and the Beast. Ironically, unlike Belle who was a very avid reader and book worm, Feyre was unable to read until she was taught by Rhysand.
* If ACOTAR was set in modern times, Feyre would love the Sofia Coppola films.
* Sarah J. Maas stated that Feyre would be sorted into Hufflepuff or Slytherin if she attended Hogwarts.
* In an interview, Maas stated that if Feyre ended up with someone from the ''[[:w:c:throneofglass:Throne of Glass (series)|Throne of Glass series]]'' series it would most likely be [[:w:c:throneofglass:Chaol Westfall|Chaol Westfall]].

'''Mr. Archeron''' is the father of [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]], [[Elain Archeron|Elain]], and [[Feyre Archeron]]. He was a wealthy merchant, that is until he made a bad decision and they lost everything.

## History
### Early Life
Mr. Archeron was born in the [[Mortal Lands]] and at some point in his life married [[Feyre's Mother|a woman]] and had three daughters: Nesta, Elain, and Feyre. His parents died during his daughters' childhood.

He had inherited generations of debts from his father and grandfather, all of which was hidden by their apparent wealth. Seeking to pay off the debts, he sent all of his remaining ships on the dangerous journey to [[Bharat]]. [[Tamlin]] once advised him that the only way to guarantee safe passage was to go the long way, but he didn't. The ships ended up being destroyed on their journey. This prompted [[creditors]] to ransack their house and attack him, breaking his knee and leaving him as a cripple. It took him six months to be able to walk after the injury, and a year before he could walk a mile. 

After losing their wealth, Mr. Archeron is unable to accept that it is gone and continues to talk about regaining it.  But when the remaining money ran out and the family were forced to move out of their home and into a cottage in nondescript [[Feyre's Village|village]], Mr. Archeron fell into a deep depression; he stopped taking care of his daughters and would sit unmoving for hours, prompting Feyre to become the primary breadwinner of the family and earning Nesta's resentment. He did manage to make a living out of selling wood carvings, but it is barely enough for the family to get by, ultimately relying on Feyre for livelihood. 

### ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]''
One day Feyre comes home with a dead doe and a wolf skin, so he is surprised and asks where she got it from. The next day, the door of the [[Archeron's Cottage|cottage]] is ripped off by a monstrous animal that is looking for the murderer of his friend. When Feyre recognizes her actions, the [[faerie]] tells her that she can die or go with him to [[Prythian]], so Feyre's father tries to convince the creature to let her go in exchange for money, something that the faerie did not accept.

After Feyre leaves, the faerie, who is the [[High Lord]] of the [[Spring Court]], [[Tamlin]], casts a glamour on the family and then on another day a man appears at their door to propose a business deal, which returns them to their old social position.

When Feyre returns from Prythian, they think she is returning from taking care of [[aunt Ripleigh]], a relative of theirs who was dying and so Feyre had gone to take care of her, but this is only part of the glamour that Tamlin put on them. Then Mr. Archeron took it upon himself to calculate the fortune Tamlin had given Feyre, though he thought it was the inheritance from her late aunt.

Later he decides to give a party in Feyre's name and the next day he comments that he is thinking of buying the land of [[Beddor Family|the Beddors]] so that one of them can build a house when the time came.

### ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]''
During this book Mr. Archeron is absent as he is on a business trip to the [[Continent]] to attend a meeting with the purpose of discussing the impending [[The War with Hybern|war]] to be unleashed on [[Prythian]].

### ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]''
In this book we learn that Mr. Archeron knew about the betrayal of the [[Mortal Queens]] and so he uses his regained wealth and goes in search of the sixth queen, [[Vassa]], who has been handed over by the others to a sorcerer who has cursed her, causing her to become a firebird during the day and mortal again at night. He agrees to raise an army to help Prythian in the war with [[Hybern]] and together they set out on the journey to Prythian. Along the way they meet [[Lucien Vanserra]], who has come looking for Vassa for the same purpose.

When they are crossing the sea from the Continent they join forces with the legion of [[Seraphim]] and ships coming from [[Cretea]] to aid the Prythian courts in the final battle.

When they arrive, Feyre sees that three ships of his father's are part of the navy, named after his daughters: [[The Feyre]], [[The Elain]], and [[The Nesta]]. Mr. Archeron captained The Nesta, though ironically Nesta is the daughter who despises him the most.

When Nesta plans to distract the [[King of Hybern]] so that Feyre and [[Amren]] can nullify the [[Cauldron]]'s powers, the King uses Mr. Archeron as a shield and then snaps his neck, killing him on the spot.

After the battle Feyre, Elain and Nesta are present at a small service in his honor and Feyre cremates the body with her powers. Vassa tells them that he has been more of a father to her than her own and says that she will remember and honor him until the day she dies.

Despite appearing aloof, Nesta is seen holding one of the carvings her father creates when they lived in the cottage of the village.

### ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]''
After his death, his daughter Feyre erects a [[Mr. Archeron's Gravesite|gravesite]] in his honor on a hill near [[Velaris]].

## Appearance
Mr. Archeron is described as having dark brown eyes, the same color as Elain's, a neatly-trimmed dark beard, and spotless face. His voice is a deep rumble. His fingers are still smooth and gentlemanly, even in poverty. After his knee was smashed by his creditors, it has vicious, twisted wounds around it. Whenever it is cold, raining, or changing in temperature, his knee hurts.

## Relationships
### [[Feyre Archeron]]
Feyre's relationship with her father was not very good, they only spoke when she said what they were going to do with what she hunted, if they were going to eat all of it, eat one part and keep the other. She also held a grudge against him for having sat by when they lost their fortune and allowing her to wander into the woods at the risk of her life.
When Tamlin took her to Prythian, she was hoping her father would come looking for her or gather a group of people to do so, but he never did.

### [[Elain Archeron]]
Elain was the only one of his daughters who treated him well and had conversations with him.

### [[Nesta Archeron]]
Nesta hated her father. She blamed him for their lost fortune and for doing nothing while her mother died of typhus. She made fun of everything he said, thinking everything that came out of his mouth was stupid and whenever she could, she moved his cane somewhere he couldn't reach.

'''Mrs. Archeron''' is the mother of [[Nesta Archeron|Nesta]], [[Elain Archeron|Elain]], and [[Feyre Archeron]]; a frivolous woman who was married to a [[Feyre's Father|man]] who was in the merchant business, so the family lived in a good financial position. She died of typhus when Feyre was eight before the events of ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]''.

## History
At some point in her life, Feyre's mother married a wealthy [[mortal]] merchant and had three daughters: Nesta, Elain, and Feyre. Her father died during the Archeron sisters' childhood, followed by [[Mrs. Archeron's Mother|her mother]] who died when Nesta was seven years old.

As her daughters grew, Mrs. Archeron unevenly divided her love and attention towards them. Believing that Nesta was more like herself, she groomed Nesta to become a powerful woman and wanted her to marry a man of great wealth and prestige on the Continent, all the while while barely giving attention to either Elain or Feyre. Nesta recalls how her mother favored her over her sisters. She told Nesta, "My Nesta. Elain shall wed for love and beauty, but you, my cunning little queen... You shall wed for conquest."

She did notice Feyre, but only thought about how her youngest daughter's painting abilities might secure her a future husband. Regardless, Mrs. Archeron did not spend much time being a parent to her daughters; while she did teach Nesta and Elain to read and write, she did not bother teaching Feyre who was too young at the time. As a result, Feyre grew up illiterate.

When Feyre was eight, Mrs. Archeron contracted typhus and passed away, but not before making Feyre promise to take care of her [[Archeron Family|family]]. She was buried next to her mother; Nesta visited her grave very often. Feyre thinks perhaps it was a merciful thing that her mother did not have to see the family's wealth crumble.

## Appearance
She had golden-brown hair like her daughters and blue-gray eyes. Nesta is the closest in resemblance to her.

## Personality
Feyre says that her mother was a frivolous woman who only cared about what others thought of her family. She did make an effort to ensure that her daughters were well-educated, but only so that they could get good husbands from high society. She was cold with her daughters, and only showed affection for her husband. As described by her daughters years after her death, she wasn’t the nicest person and possibly much of her daughters more negative aspects was inherited from her.

'''Fionn''' was the first and only High King of [[Prythian]]. He is briefly mentioned in ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'' and in .

## History
Fionn was one of the Fae heroes who rose to overthrow the Fae's ancient rulers, the [[Daglan]]. During the Daglan's rule, he kept his relationship with Theia a secret, for fear that the Daglan would tear them apart. He was given the great sword [[Gwydion]] by the High Priestess [[Oleanna]]. In secret, they planned their uprising, using the [[Cauldron]] to [[Made|Make]] the great sword Gwydion, creating a weapon capable of destroying their masters. Fionn used the Cauldron dipped sword to overthrow the Daglan.

After defeating them, Fionn unified the territories in Prythian and became the first High King of Prythian.

According to legend and [[Silene]], Fionn's reign ended when he was betrayed by his wife and queen, [[Theia]], and his general, [[Pelias]]. They killed him in a bog in the [[The Middle|Middle]] where he was bound and gagged and was taken by some kind of [[nøkk]] into the inky depths of the water. After his death, Gwydion was summoned by Theia, and [[Truth-Teller]] was offered to her by a scaled, dripping hand that rose from the bog where Fionn had died.

## Appearance
Fionn had the appearance of a High Fae male. He was handsome and broad-shouldered with golden hair. 

## Personality
Fionn is described by Silene as someone who had never been a generous or giving type.

## Powers and Abilities
Although we still don't know anything about what kind of powers Fionn had, we know he was the one to dip [[Gwydion]] into the [[Cauldron]] and supposedly did the same thing to [[Truth-Teller]]. He was accompanied by two other figures, who were veiled in thick snow. 

## Relationships
### Theia
Fionn kept his relationship with [[Theia]] a secret from the [[Daglan]] in fear that they would break them apart for sport. They eventually managed to overthrow their masters and became High King and High Queen of [[Prythian]]. 

Centuries after the Daglan's reign came to an end, they had two daughters, Helena and [[Silene]], within a span of five years. 

Fionn wanted their eldest daughter to get his crown and Theia, still ripe with power and enraged that the crown would go to their daughter instead of her, joined forces with her general, Pelias, to betray and kill Fionn.

### Helena
Fionn wanted Helena to be the one to succeed him, thinking it was time for a new generation to lead. This would later lead to his death. 

## Trivia
*He was the first and only High King of Prythian.
*Fionn was the last known person to be in possession of [[Gwydion]], which subsequently hasn't been seen on [[Prythian]] in over 15,000 years.
*Even though he took the [[Truth-Teller]] for himself after his best friend's death, he never used it.
*According to [[Silene]], the land appeared to have died alongside its king.

This '''goat-legged fawn''' is a fawn belonging to the [[Spring Court]].

## History
### ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]''
After the events of [[Under the Mountain]] the [[High Lord]] of the Spring Court, [[Tamlin]], reinstated the [[Tithe]] in his lands, all residents had to go to his [[Tamlin's Manor|manor]] to pay it on the specified date. On the day of the first Tithe after [[Winter Solstice]] this fawn stood behind a [[Water-wraith]] in line and presented himself to Tamlin after she left crying for not having been able to pay her tax. The fawn presented a basket of mushrooms as a form of payment.

'''Graysen''' is a [[mortal]] who used to be engaged to [[Elain Archeron]]. He is the son of a [[faerie]]-hating Lord named [[Nolan]].

## History
### ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]''
Graysen is a mortal whom Elain is in love with and is to make a good marriage to due to his wealth and power as the son of [[Nolan]], a wealthy Lord of the [[Mortal Lands]] located south of [[Prythian]]. His family hates the [[faerie]] who live north of Prythian and hunts down those who venture beyond [[the Wall]]. Since he is going to marry Elain, his father has him give her an [[Elain's Engagement Ring|engagement ring]] made of [[iron]].

### ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]''
After the destruction of the wall, the [[Night Court]], in an attempt to protect the human population from the [[High Fae]] invaders, decides to try to appeal to Graysen and his father Nolan, who, as wealthy humans lords, have extensive lands and wealth and a fortified [[Nolan's Estate|estate]] that remains largely unoccupied. They attempt to deceive Graysen and Nolan, pretending that Elain, Graysen's fianceé, is still human and not High Fae like her sisters, believing that she can sway her lover and his family. However, [[Jurian]] has already revealed the truth to Graysen and Nolan. Despite this attempted deception, ultimately, Nolan is convinced or rather threatened by Jurian, to admit human refugees. Graysen, however, is furious with Elain for not only concealing her true form but for failing to reveal that she has a High Fae [[mate]], [[Lucien Vanserra]]. He subsequently ends their engagement and demands she return his ring. Elain is reduced to tears, but does not comply. [[Nesta Archeron]] then smacks him upside the head, claiming that he never deserved her anyway.

Later on, Elain is lured from the safety of the [[Illyrian]] war camp by the [[Cauldron]], posing as a remorseful Graysen. The Cauldron then transports her to the [[Hybern]] war camp, but she is rescued by her sister [[Feyre Archeron]] before any lasting damage can be done.

During the final battle against Hybern in the Mortal Lands, Graysen and his father, led by Jurian, are part of the human army that, alongside an army from the [[Autumn Court]] and a small force from the [[Spring Court]], join the fray, reinforcing the battered Night, [[Day Court|Day]], [[Dawn Court|Dawn]], [[Winter Court|Winter]] and [[Summer Court|Summer]] courts. In the aftermath of the battle, Graysen ignores Elain and departs alongside his father without saying a word to her.

## Appearance
Graysen is a tall, handsome young man with brown hair and blue eyes. He has a solid build.

## Personality
Nesta described Graysen as "kind enough" which must be true considering her protectiveness over Elain. He was said to be as smitten with Elain as she was with him, however, his hatred for faeries is so great that it overcame the love he felt for her. Despite what he'd previously felt, his attitude caused him to lash out at her.

## Trivia
* Graysen's father, Lord Nolan, had built a wall around their house so tall that not even the trees could reach it. Nesta thinks that it looks like a prison.
