'''Mrs. Laurent''' is the [[mortal]] house-keeper of the [[Archeron's Estate]].

## History
Mrs. Laurent began living and working with the [[Archeron Family|Archeron family]] after they regained their fortune thanks to [[Tamlin]].

### ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]''
In order to gain the [[Book of Breathings]] from the [[Mortal Queens|mortal queens]], [[Rhysand]], [[Feyre Archeron]], [[Cassian]], and [[Azriel]] visited Feyre's family's estate. Mrs. Laurent appears when Feyre knocks on the door. She begins asking how she can help, before noticing Feyre's face. Mrs. Laurent recalled meeting Feyre before when she lived with her sisters. However, she becomes hesitant to let Feyre enter the house upon seeing Feyre's [[High Fae]] traits, and only lets her in when [[Elain Archeron]] noticed her at the door.

After the meeting between the gathered individuals, Mrs. Laurent promises not to say a word about what had occurred in the house. Later her employers ask her and the rest of the servants to leave the house as they would be visited by a group of [[faerie]]s and the mortal queens.

Soon after, she left [[Feyre's Village|Feyre's village]] to stay in a different mortal town.

## Appearance
She is described as a merry and rounded-faced woman.

## Personality
Mrs. Laurent is protective over Feyre's family but is obedient and loyal when Elain questions her decisions. She is hesitant to let Feyre enter the estate, possibly because of her faerie-looking appearance and the conflict between both species.

## Trivia
* The last name, Laurent, is a French surname.

The '''Naga''' (''pronounced: Nah-gah'') are a species of [[Faerie|faeries]] that enjoy torturing and eating [[Mortal|mortals]]. Many of the dark legends about faeries that mortals have are inspired by the naga.

## History
### ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]''
Four naga appear in the [[Western Woods]] of the [[Spring Court]] after they smell [[Feyre Archeron]], a mortal woman, and hear the scream of the [[Suriel]], which she had caught by leaving a dead chicken in the forest surrounded by a snare trap.

The Suriel tells her about [[Tamlin]], such as that he was the [[High Lord]] of the Spring Court, and is about to tell her about [[The Blight|the curse]] that is affecting the land. But she is interrupted by the naga who attempt to kill and eat her. Before fleeing the Naga, Feyre immediately released the Suriel from its snare, and he is able to escape.

Feyre runs straight towards the stream and back towards [[Tamlin's Manor]]. The naga chase after her and Feyre successfully attacks two of them. However, another naga catches up to her and violently pins her to the ground. Feyre screams in its grasp, which alerts [[Tamlin]] to aid Feyre. He kills the naga in his shapeshifter form by ripping out their entrails with his claws. He tells Feyre that he was tracking the four naga, until he heard her scream.

### ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]''
Naga attack Tamlin's manor after set of keys to the manor is found missing. It is revealed through Feyre‘s magic that the [[Spring Court Sentry]] on duty was lulled to sleep by [[Ianthe]], who stole the keys and then unleashed a bunch of nagas on the manor to gain attention after the Solstice incident in which Feyre uses her powers to appear blessed instead of Ianthe.

## Appearance

The naga are faeries with a male humanoid body, and serpentine features. They are covered completely in dark scales, and have powerful arms with polished, black talons. It's huge, almond-shaped eyes are amber in color. Their teeth are razor-sharp and their tongue is silver and forked.

## Trivia
* The naga believe in the [[Dark Mother]], who is presumably the evil counterpart of the [[Mother]]. 
* In Hindu mythology, the [[Wikipedia:Nāga|Nāga]], which means 'serpent' in Sanskrit, are a class of semi-divine beings who are half human and half cobra. They are often considered nature spirits, and act as protectors for springs, wells, and rivers.

'''Nephelle''' is a [[Seraphim]], and a cartographer of [[Drakon]]'s legion. 

## History
Nephelle was born a Seraphim with very small and misshapen wings, so she was not able to fly like the others and could not bear the weight of a Seraphim shield for long, due to this she wasn’t allowed to enter the legion, and became a cartographer instead. In this position she was very helpful to Prince Drakon in [[the War]] between mortals and [[faerie]] as she was able to find geographic advantages.

When Drakon and his lover, the half-[[mortal]] [[Miryam]] freed the slaves of the [[Black Land]] the Seraphim managed to convince the spirits of the sea to open the waters and make way for the fifty a thousand people to cross it. Miryam was in charge of supervising the passage and refused to cross until every mortal had done so, so she was attacked by the [[Queen of the Black Land]] and was stabbed with a spear in the chest.

Everyone believed that Miryam had already crossed the sea with the others but Nephelle saw the attack of the Queen and went to help her best friend. She found her injured and too weak to walk. She scooped up Miryam and flew her through the rocks and ocean bottom at an unmatched speed. Her small wings made it easy for her to navigate through the sharp rocks as the waves fell down around her when another of the army would have been stuck. The rescue is something that later no one could explain since she was not able to carry a shield three times less weight for a long time and also her deformed wings did not allow her to fly well.

Suffice to say her lover, who was one of Drakon's Generals, made Nephelle her wife that night.

Since then they have lived on [[Cretea]], a secret island whose location few people know and where mortals and faeries live in peace.

Azriel tells Feyre that the story became known as The Nephelle Philosophy, that the smallest wings might be the saving piece to a war. Since the day of the battle, the people of Cretea hold an annual flying race and the winner is crowned by Nephelle and her wife.

As Nephelle was born with very small and misshapen wings, she is not able to fly to the rhythm of others and can not bear the weight of a Seraphim shield for long, due to this she is not allowed to enter the legion and becomes a cartographer instead. In this position, she was very helpful in [[the War]] between [[mortal]]s and [[faeries]] as she was able to find geographical advantages.

When Drakon and his mortal lover [[Miryam]] freed the slaves of the [[Black Land]] the Seraphim managed to convince the spirits of the sea to open the waters and make way for the fifty a thousand people who crossed it. Miryam was in charge of supervising the passage and refused to cross until every mortal had done so, so she was attacked by the [[Queen of the Black Land]] who ruled the territory and is fatally stabbed.

Everyone believes that Miryam has already crossed the sea with the others but Nephelle sees the attack of the Queen and goes to help her best friend. Only this Fae noticed the disappearance of both of them and raised the alarm.

Nephelle finds Miryam injured and too weak to walk so she carries her and takes her flying, something that later no one can explain since she is not able to carry a shield three times less weight for a long time and also her misshapen wings do not allow her to fly well. Anyway, she manages to save her friend and that night her lover asks her to marry her.

Since then they live on [[Cretea]], a secret island whose location few people know and where mortals and Fae live in peace.

* Unnamed Paternal Grandmother †) is the sister of [[Feyre]] and [[Elain Archeron]]. They lived together alongside [[Feyre's Father|their father]] at the beginning of ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]''. She is the eldest of the Archeron sisters and [[Cassian]]'s [[mate]]. With the help of [[Emerie]] and [[Gwyneth Berdara]] she revived the [[Valkyries]], a clan of female warriors wiped out 500 years prior to the series, and became a Valkyrie herself. She underwent [[Illyrian]] and Valkyrie training under Cassian and [[Azriel]]'s supervision and is, with Gwyn and Emerie, the first females to participate in (albeit forcibly) and complete the [[Illyrian Blood Rite]] ceremony.

She was originally [[mortal]] but was [[Made]] into [[High Fae]] after being thrown into the [[Cauldron]] by the [[King of Hybern]]. She's the only being able to summon, three of the objects that make up the [[Dread Trove]]. At the end of ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'' she gained a tattoo on her back—a mark of the bargain she made with the Cauldron to surrender the power she had stolen to save her sister’s life. 

She and Cassian currently reside in the [[House of Wind]].

## History
### Early Life
Nesta was born into a wealthy high-society family consisting of a father, a [[Feyre's Mother|mother]] and two younger sisters: [[Elain]] and [[Feyre Archeron]]. Her father was a merchant, and the family in a manor near the sea. 

Nesta was highly valued by her mother, who believed that Nesta was most like her and hence groomed her to be a proper lady of high society; she taught her to be poised, regal, and vicious, to be calculating, imperious and unbending, and to use her tongue and her beauty for power and control. Her mother loved her in her own way—she believed that setting Nesta up for success in the world they lived in was of utmost importance, raising her as a noble lady, and not for who she was—a child who just needed her mother’s affection. But being young and impressionable, Nesta loved her mother regardless and did everything she wanted for her approval, since her father wasn’t a present figure in her life. 

Nesta's grandmother used to train Nesta vigorously in the art of dance. Her grandmother would hit Nesta’s palm if she made a mistake, hitting her with a ruler hard enough that they left light scars. Luckily she passed away shortly, Nesta was seen looking relieved and did not cry at the funeral, while Elain wept for their “grandmama” who was too young to have noticed how their cruel their grandmother was. 

One day when Nesta was fourteen, she danced with a duke to spite a lady that was bullying Elain, she learned the waltz the lady wanted to dance with the duke, and made her father buy her the most expensive and beautiful dress that money could buy before the ball. On the day of the ball, Nesta danced with the Duke and easily entranced him with her skills and beauty, by the end of the dance, the Duke eagerly asked her hand in marriage, to which her father refused because she was too young. 

Some time later, Nesta's mother fell ill with typhus and died, leaving the family devastated. The situation worsened when her father put all their money into a trading business across the sea, which resulted in losing all his ships; he got into debt and was hunted down and brutally beaten by creditors, leaving him permanently disabled with one injured leg. All the while it was Nesta who tried to get herself and her sisters situated with distant cousins to save them from poverty. When they no longer had the money to meet their expenses and lost the house, they had to move to a poor [[Feyre's Village|village]]. Using the only skills she had left, she tried her best to find a match for herself, settling into marrying a man whom she knew was abusive, all so that she could save Elain. If Feyre had not come back from hunting and the marriage with Tomas had not worked out, Nesta would have gone as far as to sell her body for Elain's survival 

Over the years, Nesta became even more bitter as she shrouded the loss of their mother and wealth. She remained close and protective of Elain but lashed out and berated Feyre as she hated that her hunting enabled their father’s uselessness as he refused to try and save them and take care of them; Nesta had wanted their father to move and look after them as a parent would. She believed that if they did nothing and suffered their father would step up. So she did as much as she could to do as little as possible. But ultimately their father presided himself to simple woodcarving instead of trying to find an actual job that yield stable income for them. 

### ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]''
We first meet Nesta in the Archeron cottage. When Feyre comes home with a wolf and a deer skin she says that Feyre “stinks like a pig,” and should at least try to pretend she wasn’t an “ignorant peasant.” Nesta also says she needs new boots. While Feyre thinks of the boots as a non-necessity as hers were falling apart whereas Nesta’s were "still-shiny" (likely because of the effort Nesta put in her appearance) and usable. In ACOSF Nesta recalls the boots she wore, how small stones would slip through the gap at the toe seam,  the shame she felt wearing them in public, and how they made her feel poor. Reiterating her emphasis on public appearances. 

She gets defensive and angry with Feyre when Feyre warns her against marrying Tomas, yelling at her that she’s just jealous and that she’ll have no one left to remember her, or to care that she ever existed. When they go to sell the skins to the market the next day, the three sisters are intercepted by a young woman belonging to a group called the [[Children of the Blessed]] who worshiped the [[Faerie|faeries]]. Nesta insults their ways and walks away along with Elain. While Feyre trades the furs with a [[mercenary]], Nesta pulls her away, considering them dangerous as they has already stolen from Elain once.

That night when [[Tamlin]] attacks their home to avenge the death of his friend [[Andras]] at the hands of Feyre, she tries to defend her sister Elain and when Tamlin takes Feyre to [[Prythian]] with him and puts a glamour on the [[Archeron Family|family]] so that they will think that she has gone to take care of a [[Aunt Ripleigh|aunt]], Nesta is not affected by the glamour, so she knows the whole truth. Before leaving, Feyre tells her not to marry [[Tomas Mandray]] since [[Tomas Mandray's Father|his father]] beats his wife and his children do nothing to stop it. Immediately after Feyre's departure, she breaks up with Tomas who tries to assault her and she hires the mercenary using the money Feyre had gained from selling the furs, to take her to Prythian so she can find her sister. She reaches [[the Wall]] that separated Prythian from the [[Mortal Lands]] but can not cross it.

When Feyre returns at her family's [[Archeron's Estate|new manor]] after spending time in [[Prythian]], Nesta reveals to Feyre that Tamlin's glamour didn't work on her as it did on Elain and their father, leaving Nesta with full memories of what happened. When Feyre learns that her sister tried to rescue her she is surprised because after never having stepped foot in the woods, she trekked two days there and two days back through the winter woods for her sister. Nesta tells Feyre to tell her everything that happened, and after Feyre is done, Nesta asks her to teach her how to paint.  

Nesta also explains that the reason she spent every copper Feyre got was to see if their father would fight for them, knowing that Feyre could always get more, and that she hates him for letting their mother die. When they find out about the murder of the [[Beddor Family|Beddor family]] and Feyre realizes it was the faeries. Feyre keeps thinking about her conversation with Nesta, and it gives her the courage and perspective to go into Prythian. Nesta is supportive of her decision and helps her get ready to leave. Nesta lets her know that the family doesn't need her, and they can take care of themselves, not as an insult but as a gift to Feyre that she does not owe the family anything. Feyre tells her that there is a better world and if there is a way she will come find Nesta again. She also warns Nesta that if notices something strange happening near the wall, to take their money and flee with the family, and with that Feyre departs. 

### ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]''
Nesta greets Feyre coldly, not wanting to allow Fae—even her sister into the manor. Nesta is not pleased that Feyre has been turned into High Fae and she is not pleased to have [[Rhysand]], Cassian, and [[Azriel]] in their lives because it puts Elain and her engagement in danger. She comes to be more tolerant towards the members of the Night Court, except for Cassian with whom she bickers incessantly, in order to do her part in saving the human.

In ''[[Wings and Embers]]'' (bonus chapter) it is revealed that Nesta wanted them all to starve to death to try and guilt her father into helping their family. It is also revealed that Tomas ripped the top of her dress in an attempt to assault her, but Nesta was able to escape. This incident incenses Cassian and causes him to feel inclined to defend her. Nesta teases Cassian and he retaliates, hoping to anger Nesta into opening up. Nesta then pretends that she is going to kiss him, only to kick him square in the nuts and leave him alone in the room to try and sort through his jumbled feelings.

Cassian later promises Nesta that he would be willing to die protecting her and Elain in front of Rhys' [[Inner Circle]] and two of the [[Mortal Queens]]. It is also noted that Nesta doesn't flinch from Cassian's touch. Feyre and Cassian later have a short conversation about Nesta, in which Feyre says that she believes Nesta feels emotions stronger than most people, and as such, walls herself off from the world to try and spare herself the hurt. Feyre also speculates that the only person she thinks Nesta truly loves at that point is Elain, whom she would do anything, give anything, and be anything for.

In the meeting with the Mortal Queens, Nesta holds her own, defending the Night Court's cause. She passionately defends the servents, the children and the innocents in the mortal lands, when the queens refuse to hand over their half of the Book of Breathings, she calls them cowards.

Nesta is kidnapped by [[Ianthe]] along with Elain. Once the King of Hybern's plan to demonstrate the Cauldron's abilities by turning the sisters into High Fae is revealed, he decides to turn Elain, whom he refers to as "the pretty one", first. Nesta reacts violently, thrashing and screaming, fighting to save her sister against impossible odds. Cassian, despite being critically injured and nearly unconscious, reacts desperately to hearing Nesta's cries. When Nesta herself is put in, she fights back clawing, and as she is being submerged, points a finger at the King of Hybern in a death promise, unnerving him. When Nesta emerges from the Cauldron, Feyre immediately notices that she is different, implying that she is in some way apart from most High Fae and has probably acquired some special abilities through forcing the Cauldron to give her more than it wanted as she’d fought back against it even under she’d went under. She immediately runs to Elain's side and forcefully shoves [[Lucien Vanserra]] away from her. When Lucien states that Elain is his mate, she responds with anger. [[Morrigan]] winnows the sisters out of Hybern to somewhere safe and secret within the Night Court.

### ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]''
Feyre visits her sisters after having arrived back at the Night Court. Nesta is described to be devastatingly beautiful as a High Fae. She is initially very resentful and spiteful towards Feyre and her Inner Circle, angry and hurting after the events in Hybern. She refuses Cassian's offer to train her yet agrees to practice her magic with [[Amren]]. As time goes on, she bonds with Feyre over their shared concern for Elain. Nesta agrees to go with the Inner Circle to the [[Court of Nightmares]], and after that slowly becomes warmer, though her relationship with Cassian is still shown to be rocky.

After the battle in the [[Summer Court]], when Cassian returns a few days later than his friends, Nesta is noticeably concerned and worried for him. Later in the book, Rhysand offers Nesta the job of being emissary to the human world. Right afterward when Cassian arrives, having not seen him after the battle, Nesta goes up to him and they share a moment holding hands before they are winnowed away to the [[Dawn Court]]. They stay for two days for the meeting. During the conference, Nesta defends Cassian against [[Beron Vanserra]]'s harsh words. Nesta also gives a speech angry at the lack of care for humans. On the second day of the conference, Nesta abruptly stood up in pain, this is where everyone realizes that she is connected to the Cauldron and is able to sense its movements and when it's being used. 

This connection comes in handy both when in battle and also when Elain is kidnapped. The [[Suriel]] tells Feyre to use Nesta's connection to the bones to track the Cauldron in her mind. Nesta is able to do this with Feyre's help. Nesta would use her connection to the Cauldron to scry for it and was successful in locating the position of Hybern’s camp and going as far as figuring out the size of Hybern’s armies.

However there was a consequence to Nesta touching her magic and scrying for the Cauldron as it lashed out by luring Elain away from their base and to Hybern’s camp by posing to be Graysen. Feyre and Azriel team up to rescue Elain and managed to save her and bring her back, the three Archeron sisters lay together embracing each other. At her last battle, the plan was to get the sisters and Amren to the Cauldron to control it using the Book of Breathings, but their plans would be disturbed as Nesta could sense the Cauldron’s power rallying and before long Nesta screamed for Cassians as she knew where the Cauldron’s power would hit. After she called down Cassian she was quickly incapacitated as the Cauldron’s power erupted on their armies. Nesta was left exhausted and Elain was left shook and terrified leaving only Feyre and Amren. But before long when the human ships came along with the Seraphim, Nesta couldn’t help but get emotional when she hears mention of her father by [[Drakon]] and learns that—for the first time since their family's downfall—he has not let them down, having brought troops and even the mortal queen [[Vassa]].

Cassian and baldy wounded and NNesta embraces and throws herself over him. In her anger Nesta had every intention of killing the King of Hybern with her magic as she began rallying it as Amren has taught her. The King winnowed in front of her and Cassian, using her father as a human shield. Nesta pleads with the King, and right before her father's neck is snapped, he tells her he loves her. Broken and immobilized by this, her power flickered out and Cassian lunged for the King to try and give Nesta a chance to run, but Nesta didn’t, she kneeled before her father and gave him a kiss on the brow and closed his eyes before facing Hybern who’d already injured Cassian badly. Empowered With rage and grief, Nesta erupted with all of her magic at once, unfortunately the king would winnow the last second and Nesta’s power blasted right into the heart of Hybern’s ranks wiping out a chunk. When the king reappeared, Nesta was left almost empty, and she ran towards Cassian to grab a sword and the king easily disarmed her and struck her just as fast before Nesta whirled on the ground and hit Hybern with the remainder of her magic. She rushed to Cassian to try and get him up but Cassian was too injured, and when Hybern began approaching them again, Nesta would not abandon Cassian and chose to cover him with her own body as Hybern readied his killing blow. Cassian admits his feelings for Nesta and wished they had more time together, saying he will find her in the next life, and kissed her. Suddenly, Elain appears, stabbing the King through the neck, Nesta, emboldened and protective, severes his head from his body.

In the aftermath of the war Nesta, though initially unwilling, eventually agrees to attend the meeting that Feyre has called to renegotiate the treaty. Prior to this meeting, she treats Vassa, the sixth mortal queen, harshly and stiffens upon seeing her with Feyre. When Vassa tells Nesta about how her father loved her and how he was a good man, having treated her better than her own father—and that she would live her entire life in gratitude to him—Nesta shows no reaction and instead stares her down coldly.

Back at the Night Court, Nesta is seen to be very withdrawn and upset due to the death of her father and the apology he gave prior to his murder at the hands of the King of Hybern. She is so visibly shaken that she does not even visit Cassian on his recovery bed. She retreats to her room as the rest of the Inner Circle drinks wine and make merry together.

### ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]''
After the end of the [[The War with Hybern|war]] against Hybern, Nesta's emotional health takes a turn for the worse. Her lingering trauma over her father's death and her forced transformation into High Fae causes her to become depressed and reclusive, to "feel nothing". She begins to lose control over the power she took from the Cauldron.

Nesta decides to move away from her family and live her life on her own. She moves into an [[Nesta's Apartment|apartment]] paid for by Rhysand and begins a life of solitude. She regularly goes to the [[Wolf's Den]], the worst tavern in the city, and every night she takes a different male to her bed to serve as a distraction from the pain she feels over the loss of her father, something that marks her in such a way that only she knows. Unknown to the rest, she is not able to light the fire in her fireplace because the sound of the cracking logs reminds her of her father's neck snapping. She can't endure baths as they remind her of when she was thrown in the Cauldron.

Feyre expresses concern over Nesta's seclusion, as she has been refusing invites to the [[The Town House|Town House]] and the House of Wind, and has moved to an apartment in one of the poorest suburbs of Velaris. To her surprise, Feyre finds that Nesta has become close with Amren, allowing her some small relief that she is gathering some form of 'counseling'.

Feyre attempts to reach out to Nesta and visits her apartment, but she isn't home. Rhysand uses his power to find her and winnows them. Nesta isn't pleased with Feyre's arrival, as she disturbs her card game. Feyre dismisses the males she is drinking with and tries to get Nesta to agree to attend Winter Solstice. Nesta is cold and withdrawn throughout the conversation, offering flat responses, refusing to attend, and questioning why she has to be part of the group.

Nesta is civil until Feyre tries to use their father in her argument, at which point she becomes agitated and tense, and tells her to leave. On the way out, she mentions that her rent is due and Feyre takes the opportunity, saying it will be paid only if she attends the party.

Nesta attends Winter Solstice, she is polite during the event, spending the rest of the night with Elain by the fire. Elain is the only one to give her a gift during the exchange. Feyre and Cassian both notice how Nesta looks like an "outsider looking in" yet neither of them speaks to her. While she is leaving Cassian follows her and attempts to give her a present which she declines. Frustrated at her behavior and how she keeps pushing him away Cassian calls her out and asks her to try for her sisters' sake to which she only replies with "Go home, Cassian" and leaves. After which Cassian throws her gift in the [[Sidra River]].

### ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]''
During a prologue, it’s revealed what Nesta experienced inside the Cauldron, how she was raging when she had been thrown into the icy black water that had devoured her into a sea of darkness that had no horizon. It was described as cold as a world before light before Nesta began to feel the what suddenly… the gift of immortality that had been like fire to a human like Nesta, “like molten ore poured into her veins, burning her human blood to steam and forcing her brittle bones to steel”. This violation left Nesta distraught and traumatised, even as Nesta was being transformed, she resisted the Cauldron and fought back, driven by rage and fury. She ripped into the darkness and shredded and cleaved. She promised that she’d take everything from Hybern, that they would all pay, starting with the Cauldron as she drank and swallowed power and darkness, stealing magic from it. Six months after Winter Solstice, Nesta has begun to further deteriorate emotionally and physically, becoming thinner while spending her time going to bars, drinking liquor, and having various one-night stands. It is also revealed that she feels a dark power inside of her since the War, a result of taking power from the Cauldron whilst inside of it. Her trauma and fear of what she possesses have caused her to become a reclusive foe, scared to even take a bath due to feeling like she is the Cauldron again, a fear she learned to conquer alone as she forced herself icy water, teeth gritted, teeth clenched and body tensing panic, until her body recognised that she was in her tub and not in the Cauldron, that she was alive, Immortal.

After a night spent with an unnamed male, Cassian arrives at her apartment to tell her that her presence has been requested at the new [[River House]] by Feyre. She dismisses the male and angrily goes with Cassian. 

At the house, Nesta is hurt to see that her portrait has not being included on the walls with everyone else. She expresses the disappointment that her sisters and the Inner Circle no longer try to invite her anywhere. She is meets Feyre, Rhysand, and Amren, the latter now harboring resentment towards Nesta due to a falling out between them months ago. Amren would promptly call out how she looked by saying she looked “atrocious”. Irritated by their interference in her life, she lashes out. Feyre then tells Nesta that her behavior has become enough, and she will be cut off financially and sent to the human lands, if she doesn't move into the House of Wind and spends time training under Cassian and helping the priestesses in [[The Library|the library]]. Nesta refuses, but is forced to accept once learning that her apartment was already evicted while she was gone which led to a heated argument between the sisters. Nesta asks about Elain, and Feyre explains how she has agreed and was the one helping pack Nesta's apartment up. Feyre expressed her embarrassment of Nesta and her money spending, to which Nesta argues that this whole thing was about saving face and controlling her, yelling that it is Feyre's fault she is like this and in this place. To this Feyre fliinches, but gathers herself and quickly shuts down any resistance from Nesta which led to more strain in their relationship between them when Nesta gave up and forcibly moved into the House of Wind.

After moving into the House of Wind, Nesta learns that she is unable to get liquor while there, and attempts to leave by climbing down the ten-thousand step stairs below the House, but is quickly winded and forced to return. Taken to the Illyrian [[War-Camp|camp]], [[Windhaven Camp|Windhaven]], she refuses to train alongside the and decides to sit on a rock the entire time instead, much to Cassian's frustration. In the nearby village, she meets Emerie, a [[Emerie's Shop|shop]] owner, and comes to her defense when she is being bothered by her cousin, [[Bellius]]. They get along, and Emerie promises to make some training clothes for Nesta that will accommodate the cold weather. At the library, Nesta is tasked with shelving books for the priestesses, and interacts with one named Gwyn, who is not intimidated by Nesta's cold personality. 

Realizing that the House of Wind is sentient, Nesta befriends it, asking for smutty books. 

During her next stair climbing attempt, she progresses some more before becoming exhausted and losing her footing, causing her to stumble down the stairs until she sinks her nails into the steps to stop herself, causing sparks to fly. This worries her, making her feel like it is her dark power, and she rushes back upstairs. 

The next day, she meets with Azriel and Cassian for her meal, and banters with Cassian about her bruising from falling down the stairs and her being looked down on by the rest of the Inner Circle. Cassian snaps at her when she accuses Rhys of being an asshole, to which he tells her that everyone hates her and is that what she wants? 

Cassian, as an apology, begins to train her at the House of Wind. They make a Fae bargain that if she will train with him for even just an hour that day he will owe her a favor for whatever she reasonably wishes. They strike the bargain, causing a star tattoos to appear on their backs.

In the library, Gwyn confesses to Nesta about accidentally giving [[Merrill]], a hot-headed priestess, the wrong volume of a book she requested, and fears being punished by her. Nesta decides to help by requesting the House to find the correct volume, and secretly swaps it with the wrong one while in Merrill's office while the priestess is inside and oblivious. Returning to Gwyn, the latter shares that Merrill is doing research on the Valkyries, an ancient group of warrior females who were obliterated in the [[The War|first war]] against Hybern. The two Fae females agree to keep in touch. 

During training, Nesta reveals to Cassian that she has talked with Gwyn, much to his surprise. He reveals that many of the priestess come from traumatic experiences from males, including Gwyn, whose temple was sacked and the priestesses attacked by Hybern when it was looking for the Cauldron. Some time later, something within the seventh level of the library begins whispering to Nesta, causing her to flee with Gwyn. Nesta tells her that she and Elain were Made, leading Gwyn to theorize that whatever it was must be connected to the Cauldron as well. Cassian later confirms this by explaining the [[First Gods]], creatures with immense power and are difficult to kill. He then asks Nesta about her own powers, having noticed the marks on the stairs that she left. She avoids answering, and he tells her that she is no longer human, but pure Fae, and that she was beautiful to him even as a human. Before they can kiss, she steps away and leaves. 

The next morning, Cassian teases her for reading erotica as they head to training. Afterwards, Cassian tells her about Illyrian life, his [[Cassian's Mother|mother]], and the Rite, to which she asks him if he can train the priestesses in the library so they can feel powerful and protected against males again. He suggests Feyre be one to train them, as they could be uncomfortable with him. Nesta strongly refuses, unable to face Feyre out of guilt. Cassian agrees to train them, and she posts a sign-up sheet in the library. No one signs up. 

Elain arrives at the library and wishes to talk with Nesta, who is upset to see that Elain looks recovered from her Cauldron trauma and fits in well with the rest of the Inner Circle. The two sisters argue, accusing each other of being unreasonable, Nesta snaps at her for mentioning their father and demands her to leave. Elain says that she went into the Cauldron too and all Nesta thinks about is what her trauma did to her. Cassian confronts her at the House of Wind, but she refuses to argue, and attempts climbing down the stairs again.

She makes it to a thousand before returning, where Cassian is waiting for her and they return to arguing about what is going on with her, resulting in the two of them kissing and ends with her jacking him off. She then teases him for finishing quickly and leaves him alone for the night.

The next day, the two are summoned by Feyre and Rhysand, with Azriel and Amren there as well. Azriel reveals that Queen [[Briallyn]] has allied with the sorcerer [[Koschei]] and is looking for the three [[Dread Trove]] to help locate the Cauldron pieces with the help of the High Lord Beron and return her youth to her. The Trove, which consist of [[the Crown]], [[the Harp]], and [[the Mask]], were Made, and possess great powers of control and warping, and are currently lost. Rhysand and Feyre ask if Nesta find the Trove before Briallyn does due to her also possessing power she took from the Cauldron, and can [[Scrying|scry]] for it since she has done it before. Nesta reluctantly agrees after learning Feyre cannot look for it due to being two months pregnant, and Elain was willing but Nesta didn't want her to do something so dangerous. After the meeting, Feyre and Nesta discuss the pregnancy, in which Elain discovered it after catching Feyre vomiting too many times. Feyre tells Nesta the baby is a boy and although Nesta recognizes the offering of kindness Feyre had extended, she makes comments that "having sisters was so horrible for you" and that Feyre was trying to just "keep score" between her and Elain. Feyre wishes Nesta luck in her search and leaves.

Later that night, Cassian walks into Nesta's room to find her reading, and the two talk for a bit before they decide to finally become intimate together. Nesta clarifies that it is only physical, and Cassian agrees before proceeding to orally pleasure her. They go about training the next day like nothing happened, and Nesta watches Cassian practice fighting with Azriel.

Once in the library, Nesta asks Gwyn about the Deead Trove, to which Gwyn says that there is barely any information on it. Nesta mentions the training with Gwyn, who is reluctant. Nesta begins practicing her moves in the library, much to Clotho's frustration as it could make many of the priestesses uncomfortable, but it does succeed in convincing Gwyn to sign up for training.

Returning to the Illyrian village with Cassian, Nesta meets with Emerie again, and embarrasses Bellius when he attempts to intimidate Emerie into giving up her store to his father. Emerie closes shop early and takes Nesta to the back, sharing food and drink with her. She reveals to Nesta about her family, and that her father's side of the family is looking to take over the shop since they don't want a female in charge. Nesta offers the training to her, but Emerie declines, saying she is not sure with her time taken up by the store. Nesta leaves the offer standing just in case.

Some time later, upon seeing Cassian being upset about his encounter with [[Eris Vanserra]] about Beron's knowledge of the Trove, she helps to cheer him up by pleasuring him as well. They are about to become intimate together completely when Azriel walks in, barely catching them before they separate and right themselves. She later asks Cassian to see if he can convince Emerie to join the training, which is successful. Nesta, Emerie, and Gwyn become fast friends in training, and share recommendations for romance and smutty books.

The House gives Nesta some bones to use to scry the Trove, but she refuses due to being reminded of the Cauldron and feeling unable to do it. She distracts herself with training and the library.

Merrill confronts her, revealing she knows about her switching the volumes for Gwyn. Nesta intimidates Merrill, daring her to try to provoke someone Made with the powers of the Cauldron. Merrill leaves, and Nesta's standing up to her motivates three more priestesses, [[Deirdre]], [[Roslin]], and [[Ananke]], to join the training. Though she is happy that more priestesses are joining, Nesta begins to feel guilty for advocating about overcoming trauma when she herself is refusing to face hers, and decides to scry for the Trove with the bones the House gave her. The scrying is unsuccessful.

Later that night, the Cauldron reaches out to Nesta in a dream. Nesta begins to have a vivid and violent dream that she is in the Cauldron again. She lashes out with her silver flame in terror. Rhysand works to contain her loss of control, combating her magic with his darkness. The Cauldron blocks Rhys from entering her mind, its essence preventing anyone from seeing what it is showing Nesta. Cassian reaches out to her and calms her down, giving Rhysand the opportunity to reach into her mind and pull her out of the nightmare from within, causing her to stop and fall unconscious. Once out, Rhysand was left shocked upon witnessing Nesta’s trauma in the Cauldron. He mentioned seeing and feeling everything she went through and even saw what she took from it. He reveals to Cassian and Azriel that he sensed the power of Nesta's magic that she stole from the Cauldron, and concludes that the icy silver flames they just saw was only the surface of the power, which was "pure death”, as it was speculated by Feyre before.

Nesta awakens and realizes it was all just a nightmare. She sees Cassian asleep in a chair beside her. They have a very brief encounter with Cassian giving direct, blunt answers which Nesta finds odd. Later, Nesta talks about her nightmares with Emerie and Gwyn as they train. Nesta tries scrying again this time with Amren, Rhys, Azriel and Feyre. Nesta's fingers go cold. Nesta begins to lose herself to the Cauldron/Mask as it blocks anyone from seeing what she found, and Cassian begs for help. Neither Feyre nor Rhys can use their daemayi powers on her as her mental shields are too strong. In their final attempts, Cassian talks to Nesta and then kisses her. Nesta regains consciousness and without realizing it, Cassian warmed the entire room. After that awkward moment, it was revealed that the Mask was in the Middle, in the Bog of Oorid where there aren’t no official landmarks since the place was unclaimed by any High Lords as stipulated by a law. 

Nesta, Cassian and Azriel would soon leave for the Bog of Oorid and began their search for the Mask, using Nesta’s connection to the Cauldron to try and decipher its location. Azriel suddenly vanishes and they quickly realise that the Autumn Court Soldiers are here and they seemed to be under some influence, likely the Crown. Cassian places Nesta in a tree while he goes to face off the soldier and help his brother. But after 30 minutes, Nesta starts to get worried and climbs down, she began to hear a mysterious female voice that would warn her to “run”, but while she was contemplating about who that voice was, Nesta would quickly be snatched by a Kelpie that would drag her into the black waters of Oorid. Nesta goes through a terrible time under water as the Kelpie grabs her, ripping her weapons away and kissing her. Nesta was terrified but she still tried to call for her magic to save her. While the Kelpie was now swimming through the black water, a golden thing shot for Nesta’s outstretched hands and connected with her and it would be revealed to be the Mask. Nesta donned the Mask and could instantly feel this connection to it as both her magic and the Mask’s magic melded and sung to each other, she didn’t feel pain and didn’t even require oxygen to breath. The Mask being sentient only sighed to Nesta, calling her a “home”. She would immediately begin to summon the honoured dead inside the waters that had suffered the sacrilege of being and endless meal to the Kelpie and its ilk. She called upon hundreds as she bade the dead to rip the Kelpie to shreds, giving the dead the justice their deserved. Soon after her conquest, Nesta and her army of undead soldiers would rise from the waters of Oorid, leaving Azriel and Cassian struck in awe as she held the Kelpie’s head in her hand. Cold ancient power rippled from her that caused Azriel and Cassian to bow for her in honour as they bowed for Death. She dropped the Kelpie’s head and took of the Mask before commanding the dead to rest again in peace and soon after she’d pass out. 

Nesta woke up in a room inside the Moonstone Palace but was immediately drawn to the Mask as it called to her, she was able to bypass the wards as she entered the room to see the Mask to which the others would be wary and taken aback by. Nesta would share what happened to her inside the house water and mentioned how the Kelpie actually spoke to her. Rhysand would enter her mind with her permission and translate the Kelpie’s words which confused Nesta to being a sacrifice, revealing that sacrifices were made to these creatures a long time ago and Nesta most likely killed the last living Kelpie. There was still some confusion about how Nesta even found the Mask but when Nesta mentioned how the Mask just answered the call to her own power, Amren mused that since Nesta’s magic was “pure death” and the Mask’s power was controlling Death…like called to like and the Mask recognised her and obeyed her since Nesta was even able to take off the Mask by just asking it to let go, when previous owners of the Mask just completely fell under its control and had to be killed. It was revealed they-couldn’t destroy the Mask but the Mask couldn’t be warded either so Feyre suggested using Nesta’s magic because “like calls to like”, Nesta possessing the Cauldron’s power may allow her to ward the Mask and the of the Dread Trove because the Trove is Made of the Cauldron too, and Nesta posses a piece of the Cauldron when she stole its power. Nesta was unsure if whether it would work since she felt like she failed all her magic lessons with Amren during the War with Hybern, but she would agree to try again under the instruction of Helion. Later on the day as Nesta takes a bath, her and Cassian have sex the first time.

A series of months go by as Nesta further strengthen her body and mind, training and working in the Library. She would study the Valkeryies whilst having her book club with her friends. Their training reached wooden swords in which Gwyn suggested they combine Illyrian techniques and Valkyrie techniques to create something new. Cassian is eager and along with Azriel, diligently instructs the girls along with the priestesses who signed up. The training was good for Nesta, along with the support of Gwyn and Emerie as all three of them were building each other up again and healing together as sisters, their own chosen family. 

One day Nesta is taken to a blacksmith by Cassian to show her the art of making weapons and the effort that goes into them. Nesta quickly is taken a liking too by the stubborn High Fae blacksmith who began showing her the ropes, even going as far as letting her do some work. The blacksmith began teaching Nesta how to hammer weapons and it would take Nesta a couple of tries before sort of getting a handle on it. She hammered a Greatsword, a Longsword and a Dagger and she put her self into them as she channeled her fury, her pain and passion into them, little did she know, she was imbuing them with her magic. A week goes by when Cassian is called to the River House by Rhysand to discuss the weapons Nesta crafted since the blacksmith dropped them off his doorstep in fear of them being “cursed”. They evaluated the weapons and Amren quickly deciphered that they were Made, especially after Rhysand began to unsheathe them which actively refused Rhysand’s control before he forced them to reveal themselves, and they crackled and flowed with pure magic. The Greatsword was fury and will while the other two blades seemed to have less power. Nesta unwittingly imbued her magic in the blades the same way the Cauldron Made the Dread Trove; Nesta Made a new Trove with her powers. Azriel mused that people would kill them just to have Nesta to Make more. But Amren suggested Rhysand could use these to make himself High King as the last and first High King of Prythian used a magic sword himself to proclaim the title of High King. Amren went on to say how it was Rhysand’s destiny. How all three sisters, Made Fae were ended up here for a reason. That with the Cauldron's magic, Nesta made him unstoppable, Elain was a seer, and Feyre alone doubles his power. Rhysand vehemently refuses this but even Azriel and Cassian mention that he would be a good High King, but Rhysand shuts it down entirely. They then had a vote to tell Nesta about her new abilities and the truth of her blades. Amren quickly said no as she didn’t trust Nesta with this. Cassian vehemently disagrees with this, but ultimately the vote was tied, Azriel and Cassian were for telling Nesta, Rhysand and Amren were for keeping it from her. The tiebreaker would be Feyre, who is in favour of telling Nesta.

When Nesta finds out about the truth of her magic from Cassian, she’s in shock and in awe at first before quickly releasing Cassian’s words about how “they discussed it”. Cassian releasing his error tries to rectify his mistake but Nesta was already seething when she realised that once again they were scheming behind her back, discussing her and deeming her "unfit" and "unchecked." Before she lost control, Nesta put on the perfect portrait of ice cold calm, she left the training ring with Emerie and Gwyn being worried for her. Nesta entered the House and waited for Cassian to leave before she walked down the Ten Thousand steps. Nesta felt utterly betrayed and hurt by Amren’s vote in particular, she already expected Rhysand’s vote, but Amren? She had wished that maybe there was still a chance for them, but Amren had seemingly completely given up on her after their fight. Had decided that Nesta was unworthy. Nesta lamented how the intervention in the beginning may have been out of concern and desire to help her, but this meeting was out of fear of her, hatred for her. Nesta walked all steps before approaching a door and finally stepped into Velaris. 

Nesta walked all the way through the city and to Amren’s going through her wards. She confronted Amren about her vote to keep Nesta's powers secret from her and they had a heated arguement, Feyre shows up to break up the fight, telling Nesta to go back to the house. In a moment of anger, trying to hurt Amren and Feyre, Nesta reveals the danger of Feyre's pregnancy to her and tells her that everyone hid it from her. Nesta realizes that she went too far, and leaves with Cassian into the mountains after Rhysand tells Cassian to take away before he kills her. 

During her hike with Cassian, Nesta feels immense guilt over what she did. Nesta's face remains blank and she begins to not care about anything that can happen. For the next two days, Nesta does not speak. Suddenly, Nesta faints. Cassian asks her when she last drank water but Nesta doesn’t respond, her eyes vacant. She falls asleep soon after. They reveal their feeling to each other once Nesta breaks down during the hike, Cassian tells Nesta she's worth it and Nesta breaks down and unveils everything to Cassian, about her guilt, worthless she feels and how much she hates herself and how she can’t seem to shake this cycle of hurting those she loves. She opened up about the guilt she had about failing to save her father since she had all of that power and still couldn’t save him, how he died with love for her and that she didn’t deserve that love. How she deserved nothing, how she was so tired of being in her own head and that she simply wished she didn’t exist. She even opens up about those days in the cabin and telling herself Feyre was half-wild and it didn’t matter, she expresses genuine regret and remorse, how she was willing to let everyone starve just to make her father move because she wanted their father to take care of them. She grieved that she couldn't take back the things she'd done, the things she'd said. Nesta had grown and festered in so much bitterness and rage her whole life. Cassian would offer Nesta advice about how forgiveness is something that one offers to themselves and that her tears were a good thing, it meant she cared. Nesta after finally letting her emotions lose found the courage to try. Cassian talks about his mother whose death destroyed him. They end up practicing sword fighting which Nesta does wonderfully.

They go to [[the Prison]] to retrieve the Harp after Nesta unwittingly scried during a service that Gwyn invited her too, the music lulled Nesta into a trance and using her own bones and the stone walls to scry and get a vision of the Harp. Cassian and Nesta found a secret hidden chamber within the Prison that even Cassian didn’t know existed. The chamber had carvings and a map of a cosmos that didn’t belong to their world, but in the middle of it was the Harp that laid pristine and thrumming with power, but it was warded. Cassian couldn’t break through the wards as his magic was tailored to being able to shatter magic shields and not complex spells like this, so Nesta mused that her being Made would allow her to cross the wards since the Harp was also Made. Nesta would safely cross the wards and get the Harp but like the Mask, the Harp was sentient and went into Nesta’s mind and showed her à vision of the last time it was used, a vision of a Fae people crying for entry through a portal. While there, Nesta kisses Cassian on the cheek and Nesta revealed the name she chose for the Greatsword in which she said it was [[Ataraxia]], meaning "Inner Peace". Nesta admitted that she sees Cassian as her friend and Cassian responded by saying that he was always her friend. 

The moment is ruined when [[Lanthys]] is found to be out of his cell who began taunting them and showed a curiosity about Nesta as he could somewhat sense the Otherworld magic that thrums from her, Cassian is willing to do anything for Nesta to get out and when the opportunity arises, he threw Nesta one of his Ruby Siphons and willed it to glow to help Nesta get away while he would hold the line and fight Lanthys. Nesta runs, but soon returns after refusing to let Cassian face the danger alone, using the Harp to teleport to Cassian’s side. Shortly after appearing in the middle of the fight, Cassian gets flung by Lanthys’s power and gets injured, then it was up to Nesta to face Lanthys alone. Emboldened with fury and courage and refusing to repeat her past mistake of giving up on the face of death, Nesta would unsheathe Ataraxia and execute a manoeuvre that would lead to Lanthys shrieking, it would be revealed that lAtaraxia actually bound Lanthys in a corporeal form. Lanthys now genuinely wary of Nesta and her Made blade mistakes her magic sword as “Narben” which is an ancient death sword. Lanthys questions Nesta and her magic, asking “who’s fire burned silver in your gaze” and “which death god are you” to which Nesta would try to bluff, but her bluff would strike true as Lanthys immediately recognises Nesta “as the one who took from the Cauldron.” Lanthys would try to court Nesta and even showed her a vision of their conquest that showed her wielding the Trove and her Made weapons and included a mystery object “made of pure bone” to which Nesta refuses. Lanthys would go on to snarl and try to claim Nesta by force but Cassian awoke in time and threw a dagger that lodged itself in Lanthys chest, and Nesta again, wielding Ataraxia that blazed with silver fire, beheaded him. Nesta would be shocked but also take some pride in the fact that her power was capable of this. After retrieving the Harp, Nesta apologizes to Feyre via her (Feyre's) daemati powers to which Feyre would quickly forgive since she understood Nesta and was never actually angry at Nesta.

Once Cassian is healed from the fight with Lanthys, he and Nesta go visit her old house and she tells him about her childhood.

There is a ball in the [[Hewn City]]. Rhysand has Nesta dance with Eris to try to find an alliance with him. Cassian is in awe of how beautiful of a dancer she is, he pushes Eris away (to Rhysand's dismay) and dances with her himself. It is revealed he had Mor teach him the week prior so he could impress her.

Cassian and Nesta arrive at the river house to finish celebrating the Winter Solstice. For the Solstice the year prior, Cassian got Nesta one of the first printed books in existence. This Solstice, he gifted Nesta [[Nesta's Symphonia|a Symphonia]], a device that traps music and allows you to replay it. At first, Nesta refuses the gift but then confesses she isn't good enough for Cassian and opened up about how worthless she feels and how she truly saw Cassian, not as some “bastard” the Cassian always assumed, but as this brilliant and good male. They confess their love for each other.

Nesta and Cassian meet Eris. Nesta declines Eris's offer of marriage, much to Cassian's relief. Later, Cassian takes Nesta to the Sidra to walk for a little. They talk about mates and Nesta says it just a word. Cassian then asks Nesta to admit their mates, she refuses before she genuinely opened up to Cassian about how she felt about being Fae and what the word “Mate” meant for her. Deep down Nesta was still trying to hold on to the scraps of her humanity, and that the word “mate” meant that she was truly no longer human and she was one of them, to which Cassian would say that he thought she wanted to be one of them. Nesta expressed how she never had a choice and how she doesn’t know what she wants. He ends up getting frustrated and says he doesn't like being shackled to her either. His words struck Nesta hard in her heart and instead of her lashing out with her words (GROWTH!!), she decided to call in her favor from the bargain and tells Cassian to go up to the House of Wind for the night and to not speak to her until she comes to talk to him, or until a week has passed. 

After the Valkyries are forced into the Blood Rite, Cassian worries about Nesta. Azriel tells him that if anything happened to her, he would feel it in his heart. 

Nesta woke up in the Blood Rite frightened and cold but was determined to find her friends since she was alone in the spot she woke up in in the sea of Illyrians. She’s quickly get a handle of it as she realised the Rite was rigged with weapons and other equipments, she’d make her first kill on a large Illyrian male and strip him if his clothes and used her Mind Stilling technique to calm herself and remain focus. It’d take her a few dats to find Emerie first in which she’d knees ambushed by one male before she managed to get the upper hand and stab him in the crotch. Once Emerie and Nesta were united, they slept in a cave with an illyrian named Balthazar who helped them briefly, who expressed he knew who she was and that "Feyre Cursebreaker" wouldn't forgive him for killing her sister. 

Emerie and her also manage to find Gwyn after being captured by Bellius’s but thankfully Gwyn lured a beast that attacked their campsite and saved Nesta and Emerie. Now the Valkyries are together, they chose to avoid the fight as much as possible and slept in high trees and would be involved in skirmishes if truly necessary. They fought as one and never allowed each other to face an enemy alone as they tag teamed on a group of 5 males, Nesta and Emerie and Gwyn worked and fought with such synergy and harmony that they managed to beat the males before crossing the river. Gwyn was injured by an arrow to the thigh and since the Blood Rite prohibits magic and their immortal healing, Gwyn was forced to heal at a mortal’s pace, but regardless they managed to make it at the base of Ramiel where they camped. Nesta opened up about how they’d actually win the title of Oristian if they stayed here until Dawn to which Gwyn refused and was adamant to see this whole thing through. Both Emerie and Nesta were resistant since they wanted to stay alive but Gwyn opened up finally about the horrors she endured 2 years ago when Hybern invaded her temple. How her sister protect her in her death when she got beheaded in front of her, and how Gwyn was violated sexually assualted before Azriel came and killed the commanders and soldiers to which Mor promptly gave her a cloak and winnowed her to the House. Gwyn expressed how broken she was afterwards, how she never sang again but the Library and the other priestesses helped her sing again, though she never wore her Invoking Stone because she never believed herself worthy of it, for how she had failed her twin. She shared how meeting Nesta is what inspired her to join training, because she could see that Nesta was also struggling with her own demons but she was not letting them rule her. And how her sister would have wanted her to join training. Gwyn talking about her sister made Nesta think of her sisters. 

After that, Emerie would open up about her own trauma in regards to her horrible father that abused her mother till death and forced Emerie to dig her grave on a full moon and lie about the circumstances of her death. How her father began beating her so severely that he broke her spine and left her bed ridden for a while. The verbal and physical abuse she endured for years for being a female…Emerie shared that she joined the training for her mother, so that no male could ever hurt her again. Nesta, touched by her chosen sisters vulnerability opened up about everything she had done, the bad and the worst. Her upbringing and her experiences with the Cauldron and how it affected her , about her father’s death. She told them everything so they would decide if she was worth climbing for. Emerie and Gwyn never turned away and accepted her as she is. Together they chose to climb the mountain. It was obvious that they wouldn't make it on time, not with Gwyn’s injury and Emerie’s cousin trailing them. Nesta offered to stay and hold the line but Gwyn and Emerie refused even when Emerie saw the logic. Gwyn yelled at Nesta for even suggesting it and begged her to let her face them with her, Nesta was touched by Gwyn’s love and kissed her friend in the brow and hugged her before knocking her out by pinching a pressure point on her shoulder. Emerie sighed but helped Nesta tie Gwyn to Emerie and they said their goodbyes but Emerie made sure that Nesta promises that she’ll love. Emerie moves up the mountain and Nesta turned back to the males approaching and stood at the Pass of Enalius and drew a line with a sword…her last stand. She drank the last of her water and stilled her mind, only love and determination filled her heart as she raised that sword and shield and met her enemies. She’d fight all 6 of them, but Nesta still held the line to protect her friends who had saved her even when they didn’t know they were doing so. It was then when Nesta reveals the meaning of the name she chose for one of her magic blades, Ataraxia meant “Inner Peace” in the Old Language. After Nesta killed Bellius’s group, she was tired and wounded but determined, especially after she saw that Emerie and Gwyn made the summit and were winnowed to safety. Bellius and Nesta kept fighting, and Bellius has her. Just as he is about to kill her, they are interrupted by Cassian and Briallyn. Cassian kills Bellius and then begs Nesta to kill him because he is under Briallyn's mind control. Briallyn tells Nesta she will either trade her the Trove or her mate's life. Nesta doesn't do anything so Briallyn gives the order to kill, but because she doesn't specify who, Cassian tries to take his own life instead of Nesta's. Nesta, fuelled with fear and fury, unleashed the full might of her power. The world exploded as her magic raged and flowed. Nesta let her magic rage as she unleashed every ember of it as she leaped upon the queen and unleashed herself upon her, using the Crown Briallyn wears Nesta Unmakes her. And what remains is the Crown. Nesta runs to Cassian and they admit their status of mates to one another. 

After returning from the Blood Rite, Nesta and Cassian are brought to the River House by Morrigan and Azriel, where they reveal that the baby's wings have caused a tear in Feyre's stomach, which is causing Feyre to die. And as a result, Rhysand and the baby too. Nesta, with saw the black aura of death around her sister. As Mor holds the still baby. No force in the world could stop it and Rhysand began screaming. Cassian and Azriel are holding Rhysand back, Nesta notes that Rhysand's power: and Feyre dies. 

So Nesta, driven by love and fear reached into her magic and summoned the Dread Trove. Wearing all three items, Nesta plucks a string on the harp and time stops. She hears a voice speaking to her, the Mother, and Nesta tells her she wants to feel everything, to love, to be happy, to experience everything with the people she loves. 

She tells Feyre she loves her again and again, something she's never done with anyone. But Nesta didn’t know what to do, she hadn’t mastered her power to be able to do what she needed to do, so in desperation Nesta pleaded to her whatever force would respond to her to give her the knowledge of how to save her sister. To save her sister, Rhysand, her brother, and the baby, her nephew. A bargain was struck with the Cauldron giving back the power she stole to save Feyre. Nesta keeps saying "I give it back, I give it all back," and the crown, mask, and harp fall from her. But the Mother intervened last minute and stopped all the magic from leaving Nesta entirely, allowing her to keep a little , the use of her Silver Flames. When Feyre wakes up, she tells Nesta she loves her too, and Nesta hugs Feyre, sobbing in her arms. At the end of the book, after talking to their [[Mr. Archeron's Gravesite|father's grave]], Nesta rushes to meet her sisters. 

### ''[[community:c:crescentcity:House of Sky and Breath (novel)|House of Sky and Breath]]''

: The following section contains details and spoilers for the [[community:c:crescentcity:Crescent City (series)|''Crescent City'' series]]

After [[community:c:crescentcity:Bryce Quinlan|Bryce Quinlan]] manages to escape the [[community:c:crescentcity:Asteri|Asteri]] by opening a gate to travel through the worlds to reach [[community:c:crescentcity:Hel|Hel]] in order to request aid from the Princes, she accidentally lands somewhere in Velaris near the [[Sidra River]] instead, carrying with her the [[Gwydion|Starsword]]. Azriel finds her, blindfolds her, and flies her to a residence, carrying her to the living room.

A moment later, the doors to the foyer open and Amren, Cassian and Nesta appear. Bryce sees Amren first, then has the idea to try to communicate with them in the only other language she knew, the ancient language of the Fae, of the Starborn. Amren stagers back, surprised to hear her speak the language as no one has spoken it in their world for fifteen thousand years, and the others gape. Amren notices the Starsword and nods to Azriel's knife, [[Truth-Teller]], at his side. The knife is the twin to the sword as it glimmers with dark light in answer to the sword's white light. Amren whispers that the Starsword is actually [[Gwydion]].

Bryce tells Amren she needs to find Prince Aidas or [[community:c:crescentcity:Apollion|Apollion]], the Prince of the Pit. She replies saying she doesn't know them and that their world is not Hel. At this moment, the front door opens again, Feyre enters the room, Rhysand entering after her. Bryce pleads her case to Amren and she translates to the others. Feyre then asks Bryce for her name, and Amren translates the question. Bryce addresses Rhysand and Feyre noticing they possess an air of authority, and tells them her name. Rhysand steps forward and introduces himself in the Old Language.

## Appearance
As a human Nesta was stunning, as a Fae she is described as being devastatingly beautiful, with the same golden-brown hair as her sisters and the same sharp blue-grey eyes as Feyre. She has full lips with defined features and a slender build with full breasts. She has an impeccable posture and stands very straight.

## Personality
Nesta is often read as hot-tempered and harsh. She learned to lock down her emotions under a mother who prized status over softness, and as the eldest Archeron sister she internalized the belief that vulnerability had no place in their world. She knew how to use her words to hurt, her grace as a tool. She became sharp, cold, and unyielding—and it was how moved through life. She’s honest to a fault, incredibly loyal, protective, speaks her mind without hesitation, and shows her affection most openly toward [[Elain Archeron|Elain]], which made [[Feyre Archeron|Feyre]] believe for years that Elain was the only person Nesta truly loved. It isn’t until later that Feyre understands that Nesta feels things intensely, and the walls she puts up are there to protect herself.

Feyre describes Nesta as a queen without a throne—stoic, poised, regal, and uncompromising. She confronted Mortal Queens, faced the High Lords, and held her ground because. 

After being the events of [[A Court of Wings and Ruin]], she was suffering. Trauma, guilt, anger, and fear twisted into something she refused to name, let alone accept help for. Losing her [[Feyre's Father|father]]—after spending years resenting him for their family’s downfall—cut deeper than she could admit. The fact that he still came for his daughters in the end created a guilt that hollowed her out. [[Cassian]], more than anyone, helped her confront that emptiness. For a long time, Nesta struggled with self-loathing for how she had treated Feyre, the people she loved, and her belief she had failed. 

The discipline of training with Cassian, the mind-stiilling of the Valkyries, and the friendship she found with [[Gwyneth Berdara|Gwyn]] and [[Emerie]] helped her find herself. Through them she broke the pattern of lashing out at the people she loved, and slowly began to recognize the kindness, strength, passion, and fierce loyalty that had always been part of her.

Nesta is now stronger, more self-aware, and far more compassionate than the girl we first met. She’s someone warm and deeply loving, someone who has always been incredibly loyal and quietly kind. She knows when to be a warrior and when to open her heart. She has come a long way. 

Nesta loves reading, dance, and music. These small joys helped her reconnect with herself throughout her healing journey. 

## Powers and Abilities
As a mortal Nesta was immune to the glamour placed by Tamlin after Feyre left for the Spring Court. This is highly theorised to be due to her strong will.

When she was turned fae, in her anger and rage for what happened to her and Elain, Nesta ripped a portion of power from the Cauldron. Although she never inherited the traditional powers bestowed to High Fae, the power she had taken didn’t exist within the bounds of the courts associated with the Fae. With this power she was able to blast a portion of Hybern's Army, Make Ataraxia, wield the Mask without losing herself, and use the Crown to Unmake Briallyn. Though most of her magic was gone after she exchanged it for the knowledge to save her sister (and as a result, Rhys and Nyx) from death during a bargain with the Cauldron. It was thanks to the Mother that she kept some of her former power. She is now able to still summon the Dread Trove and wield death in the form of Silver Flames, however the rest of the magic she had previously stolen is fairly unknown and no longer with her.

### Powers
Nesta possesses the immortality, natural grace and stillness of a High Fae along with their supernatural physical attributes, including enhanced strength, speed, durability, endurance and swift healing that are far superior to humans. She also has enhanced senses like her sight and sense of smell that are much stronger than a human’s.  

She has an unknown connection to the Mother, seen while attempting to save Feyre with the Trove—she speaks to the Mother saying she wants to live and love. 

* '''Pure Death''': The magic Nesta had ripped from the Cauldron had been death itself. This magic uniquely manifests itself as cold silver flames that Lucien described as “ice so cold that it burns…Ice yet fluid like flame…” that has this lingering presence of death to it. Her silver fire has always been described by characters like Feyre and Rhysand as “death made flesh”. Her flames have been seen being able to burn almost anything in their path and turn metal to ash as seen in “House of Flame and Shadow” and carve paths through wards for Azriel to enter. Although by the end of “A Court of Silver Flames” the magnitude of her magic was decreased, Nesta was still seen being able to wield her flames as a powerful High Fae, as seen when she blasted a “tidal wave” of her silver flames towards Bryce. Nesta’s silver flames also glows in her eyes sometimes.
* '''Connection to the Cauldron''': Nesta, being Made from the Cauldron and having ripped power from it, established a unique connection to it, more than Elain’s since Nesta is described to have had a piece of the Cauldron within her “like a piece had imparted itself within her”. This allowed Nesta to be able to sense the Cauldron whenever its power was being rallied. Notably, this connection does tend to have a negative effect on Nesta since the Cauldron is angry at her, especially when the Cauldron’s power is being used to a devastating effect, which causes her to collapse and vomit since the Cauldron’s “dreadful presence” brushes up against her.
* '''Dread Trove:''' Nesta has the power summon them to her location, through any wards and space. With the magic from the Cauldron, Nesta previously had the ability to use the Trove safely without being corrupted. As Amren speculated, Made beings of the Cauldron potentially can wield the Dread Trove without the  consequences and are immune to their powers. Nesta was the only one to ever wear all three Troves at once and contain their power without dying. Nesta is still able to summon the Troves but is no longer able to wield them without being consumed.

### Abilities

* '''Scrying:''' Nesta has shown some skill with the ability to scry, usually utilising her connection to the Cauldron and using bones and stones to harness that connection to find object related to the Cauldron, as Cauldron Made beings can connect with Cauldron Made objects. But in “A Court of Silver Flames”, Nesta briefly manages to scry without using the normal tools for Scrying and relied on her own bones and the stone of the walls inside the cave where a service was being held, to scry unwittingly for the Harp.
* '''Making/Unmaking:''' Nesta, possessing the power of the Cauldron was able to unknowingly imbue her magic into three blades; a Greatsword (Ataraxia), a Longsword and a Dagger. Since Nesta had stolen such a huge amount of the Cauldron’s magic, she’d developed the same power the Cauldron has to Make objects of power by imbuing them with its magic. Only Ataraxia has been further developed as it was classified as a Death Sword capable of killing the unkillable (Unmaking). The potential of the Dagger and Longsword remain to be seen. She also Made the House of Wind by imbuing her magic in it and gave it life and sentience due to an internal wish born out of loneliness for a friend. She also Made the friendship bracelets she made with Emerie and Gwyn as they act like homing devices that allow them to track each other down. In terms of Unmaking, when Briallyn orders Cassian to kill and he defies the Crown's hold to turn the knife towards himself rather than Nesta, Nesta's powers erupt. She leaps upon Briallyn and clutches her face which began to go back in time, turning young before it became nothing but dust. Cassian confirms that the queen had been Unmade. However, these powers however aren’t confirmed to have remained after Nesta gave up a most of her magic. 
* '''Dance:''' Nesta is an exceptional dancer as she was trained vigorously at a young age by her grandmother who would hit her hand her for any mistakes. Nesta is easily able to leave a room in awe with her talent. She is described as incredibly graceful, and Elain says that when she dances, she "let's the wolf out." Nesta was easily able to charm a duke with her skills in dancing and she does the same with Eris who was immediately entranced.* '''Illyrian and Valkyrie training:''' Nesta is a capable warrior in training, trained by Cassian and Azriel. She’s shown excellent skill in the art of swordsmanship, using Ataraxia as her main weapon of choice. She has also shown proficiency in knifemanship and hand to hand combat, and the use of shields fore defensive and offensive purposes. She is also capable of using a bow and arrow, as she learnt from Feyre. She’s currently being trained in both Valkyrie and Illyrian techniques. She manages to contend with the other Illyrians in the Blood Rite and hold off five Illyrian Warriors in Training, killing them, and just succumbing to Bellius. She’d also mastered “Mind Stilling “ where she can calm her mind and her emotions as heightened emotions can be a distraction in the face of an opponent. 

## Relationships
### [[Feyre Archeron]]
In ''[[A Court of Thorns and Roses]]'', the young huntress has a complicated relationship with her oldest sister Nesta. Feyre thinks Nesta was born with a sneer on her face and the two often bicker. However, Feyre still loves her sister and she warns Nesta that the guy she wishes to marry is not right for her. At the end of the book, Feyre is surprised that Nesta went searching for Feyre after Tamlin took her away. Feyre realizes that she misunderstood Nesta and the two grow a little closer.

In ''[[A Court of Mist and Fury]]'', when Feyre comes to the new estate, Nesta is cold and unwelcoming to Feyre as she does not trust the fae, but she agrees to work with her to protect the Mortal Lands. At the end of the book, Elain and Nesta are forced into the Cauldron and become High Fae.

In ''[[A Court of Wings and Ruin]]'', Nesta resents Feyre because she blames her for her and Elain becoming High Fae. Over the course of the novel, both females bond over their shared concern for Elain and the desire to protect the Mortal Lands. However, in ''[[A Court of Frost and Starlight]]'', Nesta's mental and emotional health goes downhill, and she pushes everyone away. When Feyre visits Nesta at the Wolf's Den, she tries to convince Nesta to come to the Winter Solstice party, but Nesta refuses. The conversation remains civil until Feyre brings up their father, to which Nesta gets angry. She tells Feyre to leave. Just as Feyre is about to leave, Nesta reminds her that her rent is due soon, and Feyre says she would pay only if she comes to the party. When Nesta arrives, she doesn't speak to anyone, but she does wish Feyre a happy birthday, and says hello to Amren. When the party is over, Feyre gives her enough money to pay for three months rent.

In ''[[A Court of Silver Flames]]'', Nesta is called to the River House. There, the Inner Circle informs Nesta that she will either go to the House of Wind and train, or she will be dropped off in the Mortal Lands. Nesta asks to speak with Feyre alone. While alone, and angered by the interference with her life, Nesta and Feyre argue. By the end of it, Nesta said that she will never speak to Feyre again. Later, when discussing the Dread Trove, Nesta asks why Feyre can't go searching for it, Feyre reveals that she is pregnant. Nesta agrees to search for the Dread Trove. Afterwards, Feyre reveals to Nesta that the baby is a boy and Nesta remarks that she’s “keeping score” since Elam found out she was pregnant. Later on, when Cassian reveals the danger of Feyre's pregnancy, Nesta feels panic for her sister, but agrees to keep the information from her, trusting Rhys to find a solution. When Nesta confronts Amren about her vote to keep Nesta's powers secret from her, Feyre shows up to break up the fight. In a moment of anger, Nesta reveals the danger of Feyre's pregnancy to her, which upsets Feyre. Nesta realizes that she went too far, and leaves. During her hike with Cassian, Nesta feels immense guilt over what she did. After retrieving the Harp, Nesta apologizes to Feyre via her (Feyre's) daemati powers. After returning from the Blood Rite, Nesta and Cassian are brought to the River House by Morrigan and Azriel, where they reveal that the baby's wings have caused a tear in Feyre's stomach, which causes Feyre, the baby, and Rhys to nearly die.
